title.zh: 卷二十四 馬援列傳
title.raw: 卷二十四 馬援列傳 Volume 24: Biography of Ma Yuan

sentences.zh: 馬援字文淵，扶風茂陵人也。

translations.literal: Ma Yuan, courtesy name Wenyuan, was a man of Maoling in Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan, style Wenyuan, came from Maoling in Fufeng commandery.

sentences.zh: 其先趙奢為趙將，號曰馬服君，子孫因為氏。

translations.literal: His ancestor Zhao She was a general of Zhao, styled Lord Mafu; descendants took that as surname.
translations.idiomatic: His forebear Zhao She had served Zhao as a general and earned the title Lord Mafu; later generations adopted Ma as their clan name.

sentences.zh: 武帝時，以吏二千石自邯鄲徙焉。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wu's time, as an official of two thousand piculs he moved there from Handan.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wu an ancestor had held salary rank of two thousand piculs and relocated from Handan to Maoling.

sentences.zh: 曾祖父通，以功封重合侯，坐兄何羅反，被誅，故援再世不顯。

translations.literal: Great-grandfather Tong was enfeoffed marquis of Chonghe for merit; because elder brother Heluo rebelled he was executed—therefore for two generations Yuan's line did not shine.
translations.idiomatic: His great-grandfather Ma Tong had been enfeoffed as marquis of Chonghe but was executed when his brother Ma Heluo rebelled, so the family fell obscure for two generations.

sentences.zh: 援三兄況、余、員，並有才能，王莽時皆為二千石。

translations.literal: Yuan's three elder brothers Kuang, Yu, and Yun all had ability; in Wang Mang's time each held two thousand piculs.
translations.idiomatic: His three brothers Ma Kuang, Ma Yu, and Ma Yun were all capable men who held posts at two thousand piculs under Wang Mang.

sentences.zh: 援年十二而孤，少有大志，諸兄奇之。

translations.literal: Yuan at twelve was orphaned; in youth he had great ambitions—the brothers marveled at him.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan was orphaned at twelve yet aimed high even as a boy; his brothers thought him extraordinary.

sentences.zh: 嘗受《齊詩》，意不能守章句，乃辭況，欲就邊郡田牧。

translations.literal: He once studied the Qi recension of the Odes but could not abide splitting hairs over glosses; therefore he took leave of Kuang, wishing to go to a border commandery to farm and herd.
translations.idiomatic: He had studied the Qi version of the Book of Odes but lacked patience for philological minutiae; he told Ma Kuang he would rather farm and herd on the frontier.

sentences.zh: 況曰：『汝大才，當晚成。

translations.literal: Kuang said: 'You have great talent and should mature late.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Kuang replied, 'Great talents ripen late.'

sentences.zh: 良工不示人以樸，且從所好。

translations.literal: A fine craftsman does not show others raw timber; for now follow what you love.'
translations.idiomatic: A master craftsman does not display rough lumber—go where your heart leads you for now.'

sentences.zh: 』會況卒，援行服期年，不離墓所；

translations.literal: ' When Kuang died, Yuan observed mourning a full year and did not leave the grave site;
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Kuang died Ma Yuan mourned a full year beside the tomb without leaving;

sentences.zh: 敬事寡嫂，不冠不入廬。

translations.literal: he served his widowed sister-in-law with respect—without cap he did not enter the chamber.
translations.idiomatic: he honored his widowed sister-in-law and would not enter her quarters unless properly capped.

sentences.zh: 後為郡督郵，送囚至司命府，囚有重罪，援哀而縱之，遂亡命北地。

translations.literal: Later as county mail inspector he escorted prisoners to the Director of Destinies' office; a prisoner faced grave guilt—Yuan pitied him and released him—thereupon fled as outlaw to Beidi.
translations.idiomatic: Later, as county mail inspector, he escorted prisoners to the capital. One man faced heavy punishment; Ma Yuan freed him out of pity and fled to Beidi as a fugitive.

sentences.zh: 遇赦，因留牧畜，賓客多歸附者，遂役屬數百家。

translations.literal: Meeting amnesty he stayed to herd livestock; many retainers attached—thereupon several hundred households served him.
translations.idiomatic: After a general amnesty he stayed to raise herds; retainers flocked in until hundreds of households owed him service.

sentences.zh: 轉遊隴漢間，常謂賓客曰：『丈夫為志，窮當益堅，老當益壯。

translations.literal: He ranged between Long and Han, often telling retainers: 'A man's resolve—in poverty should grow firmer; in age should grow stronger.'
translations.idiomatic: He roamed between Long and Han and often told his followers, 'A true man grows sterner in adversity and bolder with age.'

sentences.zh: 』因處田牧，至有牛、馬、羊數千頭，穀數萬斛。

translations.literal: ' By farming and herding he came to own thousands of head of oxen, horses, and sheep and tens of thousands of hu of grain.
translations.idiomatic: Through farming and ranching he acquired thousands of head of livestock and tens of thousands of hu of grain.

sentences.zh: 既而嘆曰：『凡殖貨財產，貴其能施賑也，否則守錢虜耳。

translations.literal: Then he sighed: 'Any amassing of wealth values the power to give relief—otherwise one is merely a jailer of coin.'
translations.idiomatic: He sighed, 'Wealth only matters if you can use it to aid others; otherwise you are only hoarding like a miser.'

sentences.zh: 』乃盡散以班昆弟故舊，身衣羊裘皮褲。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon he scattered it all to brothers, old friends, and kin; himself wore sheepskin coat and leather breeches.
translations.idiomatic: He gave it all away to kinsmen and old friends and dressed himself in sheepskin and leather trousers.

sentences.zh: 是時，公孫述稱帝於蜀，囂使援往觀之。

translations.literal: At this time Gongsun Shu proclaimed himself emperor in Shu; Xiao sent Yuan to observe him.
translations.idiomatic: When Gongsun Shu declared himself emperor in Shu, Wei Xiao sent Ma Yuan to assess him.

sentences.zh: 援素與述同里閈，相善，以為既至當握手歡如平生，而述盛陳陛衛，以延援入，交拜禮畢，使出就館，更為援製都布單衣、交讓冠，會百官於宗廟中，立舊交之位。

translations.literal: Yuan had always shared the same lane with Shu and been friendly—thought that on arrival they would clasp hands happy as in former days—but Shu deployed palace guards in grand array to welcome Yuan in; after exchanging bows he sent him to lodgings; further had made for Yuan cap and robe of Du cloth and deferring crown—assembled hundred officials in the ancestral temple—set rank of old friendship.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan had grown up with Gongsun Shu and expected a warm reunion. Instead Gongsun arrayed guards like an emperor, welcomed him with court ritual, lodged him apart, had tailors sew him ceremonial robes and an 'inferior's' cap, and convened the officials in the ancestral temple to seat him as an old comrade.

sentences.zh: 述鸞旗旄騎，警蹕就車，磬折而入，禮饗官屬甚盛，欲授援以封侯大將軍位。

translations.literal: Shu with phoenix banner and feathered escort, cleared streets mounting carriage—bent like stone chime entering—feasted officials magnificently—wished to appoint Yuan marquis and grand general.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun rode out with imperial banners and halberdiers, cleared the streets, bowed low at every step, and held a lavish banquet; he offered Ma Yuan a marquisate and the post of grand general.

sentences.zh: 賓客皆樂留，援曉之曰：『天下雄雌未定，公孫不吐哺走迎國士，與圖成敗，反修飾邊幅，如偶人形。

translations.literal: Retainers all delighted to stay—Yuan enlightened them saying: 'The realm's outcome is undecided—Gongsun does not spit out his food and run to greet a true knight-errant to plot success or failure—instead trims every fringe—like a wooden puppet.'
translations.idiomatic: His followers wanted to remain, but Ma Yuan said, 'Victory is still undecided. A worthy ruler would rush from table to welcome men of worth; Gongsun fusses over costume like a dressed-up doll.'

sentences.zh: 此子何足久稽天下士乎！

translations.literal: How can this fellow long detain the empire's scholars!
translations.idiomatic: How long does he think he can hold the attention of real men?'

sentences.zh: 』因辭歸，謂囂曰：『子陽井底蛙耳，而妄自尊大，不如專意東方。』

translations.literal: ' Thereupon declined and returned—said to Xiao: 'Ziyang is a frog in a well—yet presumes to magnify himself—you had better fix your mind on the east.'
translations.idiomatic: He declined and went back to Wei Xiao. 'Ziyang is a frog in a well who thinks himself mighty; focus your hopes on the east, not on Shu.'

sentences.zh: 建武四年冬，囂使援奉書洛陽。

translations.literal: Fourth year of Jianwu, winter, Xiao sent Yuan bearing letter to Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the fourth Jianwu year Wei Xiao sent Ma Yuan to Luoyang with a memorial.

sentences.zh: 援至，引見於宣德殿。

translations.literal: When Yuan arrived he was led to audience in Xuande Hall.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu received him in audience in the Xuande Hall.

sentences.zh: 世祖笑謂援曰『卿遨遊二帝間，今見卿，使人大慚。

translations.literal: Shizu smiled and said to Yuan: 'You roamed between two emperors—now seeing you makes men greatly ashamed.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor smiled and said, 'You have shuttled between rival courts; meeting you now almost embarrasses me.'

sentences.zh: 』援頓首辭謝，因曰：『當今之世，非獨君擇臣也，臣亦擇君矣。

translations.literal: ' Yuan knocked head declining apologizing—thereupon said: 'In today's generation not only does lord choose minister—minister also chooses lord.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan kowtowed and replied, 'Today it is not only the ruler who chooses his servants—the servants also choose their ruler.'

sentences.zh: 臣與公孫述同縣，少相善。

translations.literal: Your subject and Gongsun Shu share a county—were friendly in youth.
translations.idiomatic: 'I grew up in the same county as Gongsun Shu and we were friends.'

sentences.zh: 臣前至蜀，述陛戟而後進臣。

translations.literal: Your subject earlier reached Shu—Shu ranked halberds along the steps before advancing your subject.
translations.idiomatic: 'When I went to Shu he lined the stairs with halberds before I could approach.'

sentences.zh: 臣今遠來，陛下何知非刺客奸人，而簡易若是？

translations.literal: Your subject now comes from afar—how does Your Majesty know I am not assassin or villain—and treat so simply?
translations.idiomatic: 'Now I have come from afar—how do you know I am no assassin? Yet you receive me with such ease.'

sentences.zh: 』帝復笑曰：『卿非刺客，顧說客耳。

translations.literal: ' Emperor again smiled: 'You are no assassin—you are a persuader.'
translations.idiomatic: 'You are no assassin,' laughed the emperor; 'you are a rhetorician.'

sentences.zh: 』援曰：『天下反覆，盜名字者不可勝數。

translations.literal: ' Yuan said: 'The realm overturns—those who steal titles cannot be counted.'
translations.idiomatic: 'The empire is in chaos and pretenders without number,' Ma Yuan continued.

sentences.zh: 今見陛下，恢廓大度，同符高祖，乃知帝王自有真也。

translations.literal: Now seeing Your Majesty—vast breadth same tally as Gaozu—then know that emperors have their true stock.
translations.idiomatic: 'Yet seeing Your Majesty—generous as Gaozu—I know a true Son of Heaven when I meet one.'

sentences.zh: 』帝甚壯之。

translations.literal: ' Emperor greatly approved him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor admired his words.

sentences.zh: 援從南幸黎兵，轉至東海。

translations.literal: Yuan followed south on progress to Liyang—transferred to East Sea.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan accompanied the southern progress to Liyang and was then assigned to the East Sea region.

sentences.zh: 及還，以為待詔，使太中大夫來歙持節送援西歸隴右。

translations.literal: When returned—appointed awaiting edict—ordered Grand Palace Gentleman Lai Xi bearing staff escort Yuan west return to Long right.
translations.idiomatic: On his return he was named gentleman awaiting orders; Lai Xi was sent with credentials to escort him west to Longyou.

sentences.zh: 隗囂與援共臥起，問以東方流言及京師得失。

translations.literal: Wei Xiao with Yuan shared couch rising—asked him eastern rumors and capital's gains losses.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao shared Ma Yuan's couch and asked after gossip from the east and the court's fortunes.

sentences.zh: 援說囂曰：『前到朝廷，上引見數十，每接宴語，自夕至旦，才明勇略，非人敵也。

translations.literal: Yuan persuaded Xiao saying: 'Earlier reaching court—His Majesty led audience dozens of times—each meeting feasting talk—from dusk to dawn—talent brilliance courage strategy—not human match.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan told Wei Xiao, 'At court I was summoned repeatedly; we talked from dusk to dawn. His talent and daring have no match.'

sentences.zh: 且開心見誠，無所隱伏，闊達多大節，略與高帝同。

translations.literal: Moreover opens heart shows sincerity—nothing hidden—broad grand great integrity—roughly same as Gaodi.
translations.idiomatic: He is open and sincere, hides nothing, and has Gaozu's breadth of spirit.'

sentences.zh: 經學博覽，政事文辯，前世無比。

translations.literal: Classical learning wide viewing—administration literary eloquence—former ages unmatched.
translations.idiomatic: His classical breadth and skill in government and rhetoric surpass any predecessor.'

sentences.zh: 』囂曰：『卿謂何如高帝？

translations.literal: ' Xiao said: 'You judge him compared to Gaodi—how?'
translations.idiomatic: 'How does he compare to Gaozu?' asked Wei Xiao.

sentences.zh: 』援曰：『不如也。

translations.literal: ' Yuan said: 'Not equal.'
translations.idiomatic: 'He does not quite match,' said Ma Yuan.

sentences.zh: 高帝無可無不可；

translations.literal: Gaodi had neither yes nor no;
translations.idiomatic: 'Gaozu was flexible in all things;'

sentences.zh: 今上好吏事，動如節度，又不喜飲酒。

translations.literal: present Sovereign loves clerkly business—acts by regulation—also does not love drinking.
translations.idiomatic: the present emperor loves orderly administration, moves by rule, and drinks little.'

sentences.zh: 』囂意不懌，曰：『如卿言，反復勝邪？

translations.literal: ' Xiao's intent displeased—said: 'By your words—does that mean he surpasses?'
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao's face fell. 'Then by your words he is better?'

sentences.zh: 』然雅信援，故遂遣長子恂入質。

translations.literal: ' Yet he trusted Yuan in refined degree—therefore sent eldest son Xun as hostage.
translations.idiomatic: Still he trusted Ma Yuan and sent his eldest son Wei Xun to Luoyang as a hostage.

sentences.zh: 援因將家屬隨恂歸洛陽。

translations.literal: Yuan thereupon led household following Xun return Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan brought his family with Wei Xun to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 居數月而無它職任。

translations.literal: Dwelling several months without other office.
translations.idiomatic: For months he held no other appointment.

sentences.zh: 援以三輔地曠土沃，而所將賓客猥多，乃上書求屯田上林苑中，帝許之。

translations.literal: Yuan because Three Adjuncts land vast soil fertile—and retainers he led very many—thereupon submitted memorial requesting garrison farming in Shanglin Park—emperor permitted.
translations.idiomatic: Because the capital region was broad and fertile and his following large, Ma Yuan asked to colonize land in the imperial park at Shanglin; the emperor agreed.

sentences.zh: 會隗囂用王元計，意更狐疑，援數以書記責譬於囂，囂怨援背己，得書增怒，其後遂發兵拒漢。

translations.literal: Meeting Wei Xiao employed Wang Yuan's plan—intent grew fox suspicious—Yuan repeatedly by letters blamed explained to Xiao—Xiao resented Yuan betraying self—receiving letters increased anger—afterward thereupon deployed troops resisting Han.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao followed Wang Yuan's counsel and wavered. Ma Yuan wrote again and again to remonstrate; Wei Xiao accused him of betrayal and grew furious until he raised arms against Han.

sentences.zh: 援乃上疏曰：『臣援自念歸身聖朝，奉事陛下，本無公輔一言之薦，左右為容之助。

translations.literal: Yuan thereupon submitted memorial saying: 'Your subject Yuan self reflecting returned body to sagely court—serving Your Majesty—originally without duke minister one word's recommendation—left-right lending countenance's aid.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan memorialized: 'I came to your court without a minister's recommendation or patronage.'

sentences.zh: 臣不自陳，陛下何因聞之。

translations.literal: Your subject not self declaring—how would Your Majesty hear of it.
translations.idiomatic: If I had not spoken for myself, how would you have heard of me?'

sentences.zh: 夫居前不能令人輊，居後不能令人軒，與人怨不能為人患，臣所恥也。

translations.literal: To stand in front and not make men low—to stand behind and not make men high—to bear men's resentment yet not become their peril—that your subject shames.'
translations.idiomatic: To lead without lifting others or lag without lowering them, yet draw hatred without becoming a threat—that would shame me.'

sentences.zh: 故敢觸冒罪忌，昧死陳誠。

translations.literal: Therefore dare touch risk crime taboo—risk death declare sincerity.'
translations.idiomatic: So I risk offense to speak plainly.'

sentences.zh: 臣與隗囂，本實交友。

translations.literal: Your subject and Wei Xiao—originally were friends.'
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao and I were friends.'

sentences.zh: 初，囂遣臣東，謂臣曰：「本欲為漢，願足下往觀之。

translations.literal: Initially Xiao sent your subject east—said to your subject: «Originally wishing to serve Han—wish you go observe it.'
translations.idiomatic: When he sent me east he said, 'I mean to serve the Han; go and see for yourself.'

sentences.zh: 於汝意可，即專心矣。

translations.literal: If to your mind acceptable—then devote heart.'
translations.idiomatic: If it suits you, we will commit wholeheartedly.'

sentences.zh: 」及臣還反，報以赤心，實欲導之於善，非敢譎以非義。

translations.literal: » When your subject returned—reported with red heart—truly wished guide him toward good—not dared deceive with unrighteousness.'
translations.idiomatic: When I came back I urged him in good faith—I meant to lead him to virtue, not to deceive him.'

sentences.zh: 而囂自挾奸心，盜憎主人，怨毒之情遂歸於臣。

translations.literal: But Xiao self harbored treacherous heart—thief hates master—resentment poison's feeling thereupon returned on your subject.'
translations.idiomatic: Yet Wei Xiao nursed treason and blamed me like a thief who hates his master.'

sentences.zh: 臣欲不言，則無以上聞。

translations.literal: Your subject wishing not speak—then has no means upward hear.'
translations.idiomatic: If I stay silent I cannot inform you.'

sentences.zh: 願聽詣行在所，極陳滅囂之術，得空匈腹，申愚策，退就隴畝，死無所恨。

translations.literal: Wish hear go to military headquarters—utterly declare destroy Xiao's strategems—obtain empty chest belly—extend foolish plans—retire to Long fields—die without regret.'
translations.idiomatic: Let me come to headquarters to lay out how to destroy Wei Xiao; I will empty my mind of counsel, then retire to the fields content.'

sentences.zh: 』帝乃召援計事，援具言謀畫。

translations.literal: ' Emperor thereupon summoned Yuan to plan affairs—Yuan fully spoke stratagems.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned him and Ma Yuan laid out his whole plan.

sentences.zh: 因使援將突騎五千，往來遊說囂將高峻、任禹之屬，下及羌豪，為陳禍福，以離囂支黨。

translations.literal: Thereupon ordered Yuan lead shock cavalry five thousand—roaming persuade Xiao's generals Gao Jun, Ren Yu and sorts—down to Qiang chiefs—explaining fortune and disaster—to divide Xiao's branch factions.
translations.idiomatic: He gave Ma Yuan five thousand shock cavalry to win over Wei Xiao's officers Gao Jun and Ren Yu and even Qiang chieftains by explaining where fortune lay, splitting Wei's coalition.

sentences.zh: 援又為書與囂將楊廣，使曉勸於囂，曰：

translations.literal: Yuan again wrote letter to Xiao's general Yang Guang—had him enlighten persuade Xiao—saying:
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan also wrote to Yang Guang, Wei Xiao's general, asking him to pass word to Wei Xiao:

sentences.zh: 春卿無恙，前別冀南，寂無音驛。

translations.literal: Chunqing well—before parting south of Ji—silent without courier tidings.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Chunqing, I trust you are well. Since we parted south of Ji I have had no word.'

sentences.zh: 援間還長安。

translations.literal: Yuan secretly returned Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: I returned secretly to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 因留上林。

translations.literal: Thereupon stayed at Shanglin.
translations.idiomatic: He remained at Shanglin Park.

sentences.zh: 竊見四海已定，兆民同情，而季孟閉拒背畔，為天下表的。

translations.literal: Privately seeing four seas already settled—myriad people same feeling—while Jimeng shuts door resisting turning back—becomes world's target.'
translations.idiomatic: The empire is pacified and the people of one mind, yet Wei Xiao ('Jimen') bars his gates and rebels—he has become the butt of the realm.'

sentences.zh: 常懼海內切齒，思相屠裂，故遺書戀戀，以致惻隱之計。

translations.literal: Constantly fearing within seas gnashing teeth—thinking mutual butcher rend—therefore send letter lingering—to extend compassionate plan.'
translations.idiomatic: I fear the nation gnashes its teeth at him; I write again out of pity.'

sentences.zh: 乃聞季孟歸罪於援，而納王遊翁諂邪之說，自謂函谷以西，舉足可定，以今而觀，竟何如邪？

translations.literal: Then heard Jimeng returns blame on Yuan—and accepts Wang Yuan's flattery wicked speech—self thinks west of Hangu Pass—lifting foot can settle—by present observing—how after all?
translations.idiomatic: Now he blames me and listens to Wang Yuan's slick talk, thinking everything west of Hangu falls at a step. Look at the truth—how stands it?'

sentences.zh: 援間至河內，過存伯春，見其奴吉從西方還，說伯春小弟仲舒望見吉，欲問伯春無它否，竟不能言，曉夕號泣，婉轉塵中。

translations.literal: Yuan secretly reached Henei—passed visiting Bochun—saw his slave Ji return from west—told Bochun's younger brother Zhongshu—looking saw Ji—wished ask Bochun has no other ill—ultimately could not speak—dawn dusk weeping—turning in dust.
translations.idiomatic: I slipped into Henei to visit your son Bochun. Your slave Ji came back from the west and told how young Zhongshu saw him, tried to ask whether Bochun was safe, could not speak, and wept day and night in the dirt.'

sentences.zh: 又說其家悲愁之狀，不可言也。

translations.literal: Also told his household grief sorrow's condition—cannot be spoken.
translations.idiomatic: Their grief at home beggars description.'

sentences.zh: 夫怨仇可刺不可毀，援聞之，不自知泣下也。

translations.literal: As for enemy hatred—may stab—may not destroy—Yuan hearing it—not self knowing tears fell.
translations.idiomatic: One may kill an enemy but should not torment a family; hearing this I wept without knowing it.'

sentences.zh: 援素知季孟孝愛，曾、閔不過。

translations.literal: Yuan always knew Jimeng filial love—Zeng and Min not surpass.
translations.idiomatic: I know how filial Jimen is—Zengzi and Minzi could do no more.'

sentences.zh: 夫孝於其親，豈不慈於其子？

translations.literal: Filial to parents—how not loving to sons?
translations.idiomatic: If he honors parents will he not love his son?'

sentences.zh: 可有子抱三木，而跳梁妄作，自同分羹之事乎？

translations.literal: Can have son embrace three woods—and jump beam wildly act—self same dividing broth affair?
translations.idiomatic: Would he let his son wear the cangue while he plays the rebel like the man who asked for his father's soup?'

sentences.zh: 季孟平生自言所以擁兵眾者，欲以保全父母之國而完墳墓也，又言茍厚士大夫而已。

translations.literal: Jimeng all his life self said reason holding troops many—wished thereby preserve parents' state and complete tombs—also said only thickening gentry officers.
translations.idiomatic: He said he kept troops to protect his homeland and ancestors and to enrich his officers.'

sentences.zh: 而今所欲全者將破亡之，所欲完者，將毀傷之，所欲厚者將反薄之。

translations.literal: Yet now what he wishes preserve about to break destroy—what he wishes complete about to ruin wound—what he wishes enrich about to turn thin.
translations.idiomatic: Yet what he meant to save will be ruined, what he meant to guard will be shattered, what he meant to enrich will turn poor.'

sentences.zh: 季孟嘗折愧子陽而不受其爵，今更共陸陸，欲往附之，將難為顏乎？

translations.literal: Jimeng once bent ashamed Ziyang—did not accept his noble rank—now further together mediocre—wishes go attach to him—will it not shame face?
translations.idiomatic: He once scorned Gongsun Shu's titles; now he slinks to join him—can he show his face?'

sentences.zh: 若復責以重質，當安從得子主給是哉！

translations.literal: If again demand heavy hostage—where from obtain son ruler supply this!
translations.idiomatic: If Gongsun demands another hostage, where will you find princes to feed him?'

sentences.zh: 往時子陽獨欲以王相待，而春卿拒之；

translations.literal: Formerly Ziyang alone wished treat you as king—while Chunqing refused him;
translations.idiomatic: Once Gongsun wanted to make you a king and you refused;

sentences.zh: 今者歸老，更欲低頭與小兒曹共槽櫪而食，並肩側身於怨家之朝乎？

translations.literal: now returning old—further wish bow head with boy fellows share manger eat—shoulder beside body at enemy court?
translations.idiomatic: now in old age will you bow with petty men at the same trough and sidle into a foe's court?'

sentences.zh: 男兒溺死何傷而拘遊哉！

translations.literal: Man sons drowning death what harm—yet foot-drag touring!
translations.idiomatic: Better drown a man's honor than drag through such shame!'

sentences.zh: 今國家待春卿意深，宜使牛孺卿與諸耆老大人共說季孟，若計畫不從，真可引領去矣。

translations.literal: Now state treats Chunqing intent deep—ought send Niu Ruqing with various elders great men together persuade Jimeng—if plans not followed—truly can stretch neck depart.
translations.idiomatic: The court esteems you; let Niu Ruqing and the elders urge Wei Xiao. If he will not listen you may leave with head high.'

sentences.zh: 前披輿地圖，見天下郡國百有六所，奈何欲以區區二邦以當諸夏百有四乎？

translations.literal: Before spread territory map—saw realm commanderies hundred six—how wish use petty two borderlands to oppose central domains hundred four?
translations.idiomatic: I opened the map: one hundred six commanderies in the realm—how can two western pockets stand against a hundred and four in the heartland?'

sentences.zh: 春卿事季孟，外有君臣之義，內有朋友之道。

translations.literal: Chunqing serves Jimeng—outward has lord-minister righteousness—inward has friend way.
translations.idiomatic: You serve him as subject without and as friend within.'

sentences.zh: 言君臣邪，固當諫爭；

translations.literal: Speaking lord-minister—fixed ought remonstrate contend;
translations.idiomatic: As minister you should remonstrate;

sentences.zh: 語朋友邪，應有切磋。

translations.literal: speaking friends—ought have polish cut.
translations.idiomatic: as friend you should debate frankly.'

sentences.zh: 豈有知其無成，而但萎腇咋舌，叉手從族乎？

translations.literal: How can there be knowing his no success—yet only wilt—click tongue—fold hands follow clan destruction?
translations.idiomatic: Can you watch him fail, bite your tongue, and fold your arms while kin die?'

sentences.zh: 及今成計，殊尚善也；

translations.literal: Reach now complete plan—still quite good;
translations.idiomatic: Settle with him now while there is time;

sentences.zh: 過是，欲少味矣。

translations.literal: pass this—wishes few flavor.
translations.idiomatic: wait longer and the taste turns bitter.'

sentences.zh: 且來君叔天下信士，朝廷重之，其意依依，常獨為西州言。

translations.literal: Moreover Lai Junshu realm's trusted knight—court esteems him—his intent lingering—often alone speaks for western province.'
translations.idiomatic: Lai Xi is the empire's honest broker and the court listens; he still speaks for the west.'

sentences.zh: 援商朝廷，尤欲立信於此，必不負約。

translations.literal: Yuan counsels court—especially wishes establish trust here—must not break covenant.'
translations.idiomatic: I urge the court to keep faith with you; it will not break its word.'

sentences.zh: 援不得久留，願急賜報。

translations.literal: Yuan cannot long remain—wishes urgent grant reply.'
translations.idiomatic: I cannot stay long—send answer quickly.'

sentences.zh: 廣竟不答。

translations.literal: Guang ultimately did not reply.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Guang never answered.

sentences.zh: 八年，帝自西征囂，至漆，諸將多以王師之重，不宜遠入險阻，計豫未決。

translations.literal: Eighth year—emperor personally west campaigned Xiao—reached Qi—various generals mostly thought royal army's weight—ought not far enter danger barrier—plan hesitated not decided.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year Guangwu marched west against Wei Xiao and reached Qi. Most generals argued that an imperial host should not plunge into defiles; the plan stalled.

sentences.zh: 會召援，夜至，帝大喜，引入，具以群議質之。

translations.literal: Meeting summoned Yuan—night arrived—emperor greatly pleased—led in—fully with crowd deliberations question him.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan was summoned; he arrived by night. The delighted emperor brought him in and tested every objection of the council.

sentences.zh: 援因說隗囂將帥有土崩之勢，兵進有必破之狀。

translations.literal: Yuan thereupon explained Wei Xiao generals had earth-collapse momentum—army advance had certain-break aspect.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan showed that Wei's generals were ready to collapse and that Han could break them at a stroke.

sentences.zh: 又於帝前聚米為山谷，指畫形勢，開示眾軍所從道徑往來，分析曲折，昭然可曉。

translations.literal: Moreover before emperor piled rice as mountains valleys—pointed painted terrain—opened showed hosts' paths coming going—analyzed twists turns—brilliantly could understand.
translations.idiomatic: Before the emperor he heaped rice into a relief map, traced the valleys, and showed each column's route until all saw it clearly.

sentences.zh: 帝曰：『虜在吾目中矣。

translations.literal: Emperor said: 'The barbarians are in my eyes.'
translations.idiomatic: 'The enemy lies plain before my eyes,' said the emperor.

sentences.zh: 』明旦，遂進軍至第一，囂眾大潰。

translations.literal: Next dawn thereupon advanced army reached Diyiqu—Xiao's crowds greatly routed.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn the army pushed to Diyiqu and routed Wei Xiao's host.

sentences.zh: 九年，拜援為太中大夫，副來歙監諸將平涼州。

translations.literal: Ninth year—appointed Yuan Grand Palace Gentleman—deputy Lai Xi supervise various generals pacify Liang province.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year Ma Yuan became grand palace gentleman under Lai Xi to oversee the pacification of Liang province.

sentences.zh: 自王莽末，西羌寇邊，遂入居塞內，金城屬縣多為虜有。

translations.literal: From Wang Mang's end—western Qiang raided frontier—thereupon entered dwelling inside passes—Jincheng dependent counties mostly by captives possessed.
translations.idiomatic: Since Wang Mang's fall the western Qiang had raided the frontier, moved inside the passes, and seized much of Jincheng.

sentences.zh: 來歙奏言隴西侵殘，非馬援莫能定。

translations.literal: Lai Xi memorialized saying Longxi invaded devastated—without Ma Yuan none can settle.
translations.idiomatic: Lai Xi reported that Longxi lay in ruins and only Ma Yuan could stabilize it.

sentences.zh: 十一年夏，璽書拜援隴西太守。

translations.literal: Eleventh year summer—imperial letter appointed Yuan Longxi governor.
translations.idiomatic: That summer an imperial rescript named Ma Yuan governor of Longxi.

sentences.zh: 援乃發步騎三千人，擊破先零羌於臨洮，斬首數百級，獲馬、牛、羊萬餘頭。

translations.literal: Yuan thereupon deployed foot and horse three thousand—broke Xianling Qiang at Lintao—beheaded several hundred grades—obtained horses oxen sheep over ten thousand head.
translations.idiomatic: He marched three thousand infantry and cavalry, defeated the Xianling Qiang at Lintao, took several hundred heads, and captured more than ten thousand livestock.

sentences.zh: 守塞諸羌八千餘人詣援降，詣種有數萬，屯聚寇抄，拒浩隘。

translations.literal: Pass-guarding various Qiang over eight thousand came to Yuan surrender—those sorts numbered tens of thousands—gathered raiding copying—blocking Hao'ai.
translations.idiomatic: More than eight thousand Qiang along the passes surrendered; tens of thousands more clustered at Hao Pass raiding and looting.

sentences.zh: 援與揚武將軍馬成擊之。

translations.literal: Yuan with General Who Displays Might Ma Cheng struck them.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan and Ma Cheng, general who displays might, attacked them.

sentences.zh: 羌因將其妻子輜重移阻於允吾谷，援乃潛行間道，掩赴其營。

translations.literal: Qiang thereupon led wives children baggage shift block at Yunwu Valley—Yuan thereupon secretly traveled byway—surprise rushed their camp.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang moved families and supplies into Yunwu Gorge; Ma Yuan took a hidden path and stormed their camp.

sentences.zh: 羌大驚壞，復遠徙唐翼谷中，援復追討之。

translations.literal: Qiang greatly alarmed broke—again far moved Tangyi Valley center—Yuan again pursued attacked.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang panicked and fled deep into Tangyi Valley; Ma Yuan pursued again.

sentences.zh: 羌引精兵聚北山上，援陳軍向山，而分遣數百騎繞襲其後，乘夜放火，擊鼓叫噪，虜遂大潰，凡斬首千餘級。

translations.literal: Qiang led picked troops gather north mountain—Yuan arrayed army facing mountain—while separately dispatched several hundred riders circle strike behind—using night set fires—beat drums shout clamor—captives thereupon greatly routed—altogether beheaded over thousand grades.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang massed picked warriors on the northern heights. Ma Yuan faced them while sending hundreds of riders around their rear; at night his men lit fires, beat drums, and shouted until the enemy broke, losing over a thousand heads.

sentences.zh: 援以兵少，不得窮追，收其谷糧畜產而還。

translations.literal: Yuan because troops few—could not exhaust pursue—gathered their grain livestock property returned.
translations.idiomatic: Too few to pursue far, he seized their grain and stock and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 援中矢貫脛，帝以璽書勞之，賜牛、羊數千頭，援盡班諸賓客。

translations.literal: Yuan struck by arrow piercing shin—emperor by imperial letter consoled him—bestowed oxen sheep several thousand head—Yuan all distributed various retainers.
translations.idiomatic: An arrow pierced Ma Yuan's shin; the emperor consoled him by rescript and sent thousands of head of livestock, which he gave entirely to his clients.

sentences.zh: 是時，朝臣以金城破羌之西，塗遠多寇，議欲棄之。

translations.literal: At this time—court ministers because Jincheng west of Poluo Qiang—road far many bandits—debated wishing abandon it.
translations.idiomatic: Court officials argued that land west of Poluo Qiang in Jincheng was distant and dangerous and should be abandoned.

sentences.zh: 援上言，破羌以西城多完牢，易可依固；

translations.literal: Yuan submitted memorial—west of Poluo Qiang cities mostly intact firm—easy thereby rely defend;
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan replied that west of Poluo the towns stood strong and could be held;

sentences.zh: 其田土肥壤，灌溉流通。

translations.literal: its fields soil fertile loam—irrigation flowing through.
translations.idiomatic: the soil was rich and irrigation worked;

sentences.zh: 如令羌在湟中，則為害不休，不可棄也。

translations.literal: if allow Qiang in Huangzhong—then harm without cease—cannot abandon.
translations.idiomatic: and if the Qiang held the Huang river basin the harm would never end—the land must not be surrendered.

sentences.zh: 帝然之，於是詔武威太守，令悉還金城客民。

translations.literal: Emperor approved—thereupon edict Wuwei governor—ordered all return Jincheng guest people.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed and ordered the Wuwei governor to send every refugee back to Jincheng.

sentences.zh: 歸者三千餘口，使各反舊邑。

translations.literal: Returnees over three thousand mouths—ordered each return old settlement.
translations.idiomatic: More than three thousand people came home to their old towns.

sentences.zh: 援奏為置長吏，繕城郭，起塢候，開導水田，勸以耕牧，郡中樂業。

translations.literal: Yuan memorialized to appoint magistrates—repair city walls—raise fort beacons—open guide wet fields—urge by farming herding—commandery inside joyful occupation.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan had magistrates appointed, walls and watchtowers rebuilt, irrigation opened, and farming encouraged until the commandery prospered.

sentences.zh: 又遣羌豪楊封譬說塞外羌，皆來和親。

translations.literal: Also sent Qiang chief Yang Feng explain persuade beyond-pass Qiang—all came peace kinship.
translations.idiomatic: He sent the Qiang elder Yang Feng to win over the tribes beyond the passes; all sued for peace.

sentences.zh: 又武都氐人背公孫述來降者，援皆上復其侯王君長，賜印綬，帝悉從之。

translations.literal: Also Wudu Di people who turned from Gongsun Shu to surrender—Yuan all memorialized restore their marquis king chiefs—bestow seals cords—emperor all followed.
translations.idiomatic: For Wudu Di who abandoned Gongsun Shu he restored their titles and seals; the emperor approved every request.

sentences.zh: 乃罷馬成軍。

translations.literal: Thereupon dismissed Ma Cheng's army.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Cheng's army was then disbanded.

sentences.zh: 十三年，武都參狼羌與塞外諸種為寇，殺長吏。

translations.literal: Thirteenth year—Wudu Senlang Qiang with beyond-pass various sorts raided—killed chief clerks.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirteenth year the Senlang Qiang of Wudu joined outer tribes in killing officials.

sentences.zh: 援將四千餘人擊之，至氐道縣，羌在山上，授軍據便地，奪其水草，不與戰，羌遂窮困，豪帥數十萬戶亡出塞，諸種萬餘人悉降，於是隴右清靜。

translations.literal: Yuan led over four thousand struck them—reached Didao county—Qiang on mountain—Yuan's army seized favorable ground—deprived their water grass—did not give battle—Qiang thereupon destitute—powerful chiefs several hundred thousand households fled out passes—various sorts over ten thousand all surrendered—thereupon Long right clear quiet.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan took four thousand men to Didao. The Qiang held the heights; Ma Yuan cut their water and forage without fighting until they broke. Hundreds of thousands fled beyond the passes and over ten thousand surrendered, quieting Longyou.

sentences.zh: 援務開恩信，寬以待下，任吏以職，但總大體而已。

translations.literal: Yuan strove open kindness trust—lenient thereby treat subordinates—assign clerks by duty—but grasp great outline only.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan ruled with kindness and leniency, delegated routine business, and kept only the big decisions.

sentences.zh: 賓客故人，日滿其門。

translations.literal: Retainers old friends—daily filled his gate.
translations.idiomatic: Clients and old friends crowded his gate daily.

sentences.zh: 諸曹時白外事，援輒曰：『此丞、掾之任，何足相煩。

translations.literal: Various bureaus sometimes reported outer affairs—Yuan always said: 'This is assistant and clerk's duty—how worth troubling each other.
translations.idiomatic: When aides brought petty frontier news he said, 'That belongs to my assistants—do not bother me.

sentences.zh: 頗哀老子，使得遨遊。

translations.literal: Rather pity old man—allow roam free.
translations.idiomatic: Spare an old man and let him wander free.

sentences.zh: 若大姓侵小民，黠羌欲旅距，此乃太守事耳。

translations.literal: If great clan invades small people—cunning Qiang wish band together resist—this then is governor's affair.
translations.idiomatic: Great houses bullying commoners or Qiang plotting rebellion—that is the governor's business.

sentences.zh: 』傍縣嘗有報仇者，吏民驚言羌反，百姓奔入城郭。

translations.literal: Neighboring county once had revenge killers—clerks people alarmed said Qiang rebel—common people ran into city walls.
translations.idiomatic: When feuding killers in a neighboring county sparked rumors of a Qiang revolt, people fled into the cities.

sentences.zh: 狄道長詣門，請閉城發兵。

translations.literal: Didao chief came gate—requested close city deploy troops.
translations.idiomatic: The Didao magistrate asked to seal the gates and call out troops.

sentences.zh: 援時與賓客飲，大笑曰：『燒虜何敢復犯我。

translations.literal: Yuan then with retainers drinking—great laughed saying: 'Burn barbarians how dare again invade me.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan, drinking with guests, laughed: 'Those ridge-bandits would not dare touch us.

sentences.zh: 曉狄道長歸守寺舍，良怖急者，可床下伏。

translations.literal: Tell Didao chief return guard office—those truly terrified urgent—may hide under bed.
translations.idiomatic: Tell the magistrate to go back to his yamen; anyone truly frightened may crawl under his couch.

sentences.zh: 』後稍定，郡中服之。

translations.literal: Afterward gradually settled—commandery inside submitted him.
translations.idiomatic: Calm returned and the commandery respected him.

sentences.zh: 視事六年，徵入為虎賁中郎將。

translations.literal: Held office six years—summoned in as Colonel of Gentlemen for All Purposes.
translations.idiomatic: After six years he was recalled as colonel of the gentlemen for all purposes.

sentences.zh: 初，援在隴西上書，言宜如舊鑄五銖錢。

translations.literal: Initially Yuan in Longxi submitted memorial—said ought as former cast five zhu cash.
translations.idiomatic: While in Longxi he had asked to resume casting the old five-zhu coin.

sentences.zh: 事下三府，三府奏以為未可許，事遂寢。

translations.literal: Affair sent Three Offices—Three Offices memorialized thought not yet permitted—affair thereupon slept.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Offices judged against it and the matter slept.

sentences.zh: 乃援還，從公府求得前奏，難十餘條，乃隨牒解釋，更具表言。

translations.literal: When Yuan returned—from duke offices sought obtain former memorial—refuted over ten articles—thereupon followed documents explained—again submitted memorial speak.
translations.idiomatic: Back at court he retrieved his old memorial, answered each objection, and resubmitted.

sentences.zh: 帝從之，天下賴其便。

translations.literal: Emperor followed—all realm relied its convenience.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed and the empire benefited.

sentences.zh: 援自還京師，數被進見。

translations.literal: Yuan self returned capital—several times received advance audiences.
translations.idiomatic: After returning to the capital he was summoned often.

sentences.zh: 為人明須發，眉目如畫，閑於進對，尤善述前世行事。

translations.literal: As man bright beard hair—eyebrows eyes like painting—calm in advancement reply—especially good narrating former generations' deeds.
translations.idiomatic: He had a striking beard and painted brows, spoke easily at audience, and loved recounting old stories.

sentences.zh: 每言及三輔長者，下至閭里少年，皆可觀聽。

translations.literal: Each speech reaching Three Adjuncts elders—down to lane hamlet youths—all could observe listen.
translations.idiomatic: Whether telling of capital elders or village youths, everyone listened.

sentences.zh: 自皇太子、諸王侍聞者，莫不屬耳忘倦。

translations.literal: From heir-apparent—various kings attendants hearing—none not strain ear forget weary.
translations.idiomatic: Heir-apparent and princes hung on his words.

sentences.zh: 又善兵策，帝常言『伏波論兵，與我意合』，每有所謀，未嘗不用。

translations.literal: Also good military stratagem—emperor often said 'Wave-queller discusses arms—with my intent agrees'—each time had plans—never not used.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said Ma Yuan's military counsel matched his own and always acted on it.

sentences.zh: 初，卷人維汜，訞言稱神，有弟子數百人，坐伏誅。

translations.literal: Initially Juan man Wei Si—false words called spirit—had disciples several hundred—sat executed.
translations.idiomatic: A Juan native named Wei Si had posed as a god with hundreds of disciples and was executed.

sentences.zh: 後其弟子李廣等宣言汜神化不死，以誑惑百姓。

translations.literal: After his disciples Li Guang etc. proclaimed Si spirit-transformed undying—to delude common people.
translations.idiomatic: His followers Li Guang and others claimed Wei Si had become immortal to deceive the people.

sentences.zh: 十七年，遂共聚會徒黨，攻沒完城，殺完侯劉閔，自稱『南嶽大師』。

translations.literal: Seventeenth year—thereupon together gathered bands—attacked sank Wancheng—killed Marquis of Wan Liu Min—self styled 'South March Grand Master.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventeenth year they seized Wancheng, killed Marquis Liu Min, and Li Guang styled himself South March Grand Master.

sentences.zh: 遣謁者張宗將兵數千人討之，復為廣所敗。

translations.literal: Sent usher Zhang Zong led troops several thousand strike them—again by Guang defeated.
translations.idiomatic: Usher Zhang Zong attacked with thousands and was beaten by Li Guang.

sentences.zh: 於是使援發諸郡兵，合萬餘人，擊破廣等，斬之。

translations.literal: Thereupon ordered Yuan deploy various commandery troops—combined over ten thousand—broke Guang etc.—beheaded them.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan was ordered to raise ten thousand men from several commanderies, crushed Li Guang, and executed him.

sentences.zh: 又交阯女子徵側及女弟徵貳反，攻沒其郡，九真、日南、合浦蠻夷皆應之，寇略嶺外六十餘城，側自立為王。

translations.literal: Further Jiaozhi woman Zheng Ce and younger sister Zheng Er rebelled—attacked sank their commandery—Jiuzhen, Rinan, Hepu barbarians all responded—raided swept beyond ranges sixty cities—Ce self established king.
translations.idiomatic: The sisters Zheng Ce and Zheng Er of Jiaozhi rebelled, seized their commandery, won Jiuzhen, Rinan, and Hepu, raided sixty cities south of the ranges, and Zheng Ce declared herself queen.

sentences.zh: 於是璽書拜援伏波將軍，以扶樂侯劉隆為副，督樓船將軍段誌等南擊交阯。

translations.literal: Thereupon imperial letter appointed Yuan Wave-queller general—with Fule marquis Liu Long as deputy—supervise tower-ship general Duan Zhi etc. south strike Jiaozhi.
translations.idiomatic: An imperial rescript named Ma Yuan general who calms the waves with Liu Long of Fule as deputy and ordered him south with Duan Zhi's fleet against Jiaozhi.

sentences.zh: 軍至合浦而誌病卒，詔援並將其兵。

translations.literal: Army reached Hepu—Zhi fell ill died—edict Yuan simultaneously commanded his troops.
translations.idiomatic: At Hepu Duan Zhi died; Ma Yuan absorbed his command.

sentences.zh: 遂緣海而進，隨山刊道千餘里。

translations.literal: Thereupon followed coast advanced—along mountains cut road over thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: They marched along the coast and carved roads for more than a thousand li through the hills.

sentences.zh: 十八年春，軍至浪泊上，與賊戰，破之，斬首數千級，降者萬餘人。

translations.literal: Eighteenth year spring—army reached Langbo heights—battled bandits—broke them—beheaded several thousand grades—surrendered over ten thousand.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of the eighteenth year they fought at Langbo, won a victory with thousands of heads and ten thousand surrenders.

sentences.zh: 援追徵側等至禁谿，數敗之，賊遂散走。

translations.literal: Yuan pursued Zheng Ce etc. to Forbidden Stream—several times defeated them—bandits thereupon scattered fled.
translations.idiomatic: He pursued the Zheng sisters to Forbidden Stream, routed them repeatedly, and they fled.

sentences.zh: 明年正月，斬徵側、徵貳，傳首洛陽。

translations.literal: Next year first month—executed Zheng Ce, Zheng Er—sent heads Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: The next New Year he beheaded both sisters and sent the heads to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 封援為新息侯，食邑三千戶。

translations.literal: Enfeoffed Yuan as marquis of Xixin—food fief three thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan was enfeoffed as marquis of Xixin with three thousand households.

sentences.zh: 援乃擊牛釃酒，勞饗軍士。

translations.literal: Yuan thereupon slaughtered oxen strained wine—reward feast soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: He slaughtered cattle and poured wine to feast the troops.

sentences.zh: 從容謂官屬曰：『吾從弟少遊常哀吾慷慨多大志，曰：「士生一世，但取衣食裁足，乘下澤車，禦款段馬，為郡掾史，守墳墓，鄉里稱善人，斯可矣。

translations.literal: Calmly told officials: 'My cousin Shaoyou often pitied my fervor great ambitions—said: «Man born one generation—but take clothes food just enough—ride low swamp cart—drive slow nag horse—be commandery clerk—guard tombs—village praised good man—this enough.
translations.idiomatic: He told his staff: 'My cousin Shaoyou used to say I should content myself with a clerk's post and local reputation.'

sentences.zh: 致求盈余，但自苦耳。

translations.literal: Seek surplus—but self bitter ear.
translations.idiomatic: Chasing more only brings pain.'

sentences.zh: 」當吾在浪泊、西裏間，虜未滅之時，下潦上霧，毒氣重蒸，仰視飛鳶跕跕墮水中，臥念少遊平生時語，何可得也！

translations.literal: » When I was between Langbo and Xili—captives not yet extinct—below floods above fog—poison vapor layered steam—look up flying kite flutter fall in water—lying thought Shaoyou lifetime words—how could obtain!
translations.idiomatic: »Yet between Langbo and Xili, with fever mist and poison air, watching kites drop into the swamps, how could I reach his humble ideal!

sentences.zh: 今賴士大夫之力，被蒙大恩，猥先諸君紆佩金紫，且喜且慚。

translations.literal: Now relying scholars officers' force—covered receive great grace—humbly before you gentlemen wear gold purple—both joy and shame.
translations.idiomatic: Now, thanks to you, I wear gold and purple—both proud and humbled.'

sentences.zh: 』吏士皆伏稱萬歲。

translations.literal: ' Officers soldiers all bowed called myriad years.
translations.idiomatic: The troops shouted long life.

sentences.zh: 援將樓船大小二千餘艘，戰士二萬餘人，進擊九真賊徵側餘黨都羊等，自無功至居風，斬獲五千餘人，嶠南悉平。

translations.literal: Yuan led tower ships large small over two thousand—warriors over twenty thousand—advanced struck Jiuzhen bandits Zheng Ce remnant clique Du Yang etc.—from Wugong to Jufeng—beheaded captured over five thousand—south of ranges all pacified.
translations.idiomatic: With two thousand ships and twenty thousand men he cleared Jiuzhen of Du Yang's remnant bands from Wugong to Jufeng, taking five thousand heads and pacifying the south.

sentences.zh: 援奏言西於縣戶有三萬二千，遠界去庭千餘里，請分為封溪、望海二縣，許之。

translations.literal: Yuan memorialized saying Xiyu county households thirty-two thousand—distant border thousand li from court—requested divide into Fengxi, Wanghai two counties—permitted.
translations.idiomatic: He asked to split Xiyu into Fengxi and Wanghai; the court agreed.

sentences.zh: 援所過輒為郡縣治城郭，穿渠灌溉，以利其民。

translations.literal: Where Yuan passed—then for commanderies counties repair walls—dig canals irrigation—thereby profit their people.
translations.idiomatic: Wherever he marched he walled towns, dug canals, and helped the people.

sentences.zh: 條奏越律與漢律駁者十餘事，與越人申明舊制以約束之，自後駱越奉行馬將軍故事。

translations.literal: Itemized memorialized Yue laws clashing Han laws over ten matters—with Yue people clarify old systems to bind them—afterward Luoyue followed General Ma precedent.
translations.idiomatic: He harmonized Yue and Han law and the Luoyue thereafter kept General Ma's regulations.

sentences.zh: 二十年秋，振旅還京師，軍吏經瘴疫死者十四五。

translations.literal: Twentieth year autumn—roused army returned capital—officers clerks passing miasma epidemic died four or five in ten.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of the twentieth year he brought the army home; miasma killed four or five tenths of the men.

sentences.zh: 賜援兵車一乘，朝見位次九卿。

translations.literal: Bestowed Yuan war chariot one—court audience rank order Nine Ministers.
translations.idiomatic: He received a war chariot and audience rank beside the Nine Ministers.

sentences.zh: 援好騎，善別名馬，於交阯得駱越銅鼓，乃鑄為馬式，還上之。

translations.literal: Yuan loved riding—good distinguishing famous horses—in Jiaozhi obtained Luoyue bronze drum—thereupon cast as horse model—returned presented upward.
translations.idiomatic: He loved horses, judged them expertly, and cast a bronze horse model from a Luoyue drum for the throne.

sentences.zh: 因表曰：『夫行天莫如龍，行地莫如馬。

translations.literal: Thereupon memorial said: 'He who travels heaven none like dragon—travels earth none like horse.
translations.idiomatic: His memorial read: 'Dragon matches heaven; horse matches earth.'

sentences.zh: 馬者甲兵之本。

translations.literal: Horse is armor weapons' root.
translations.idiomatic: Horses are the root of arms.

sentences.zh: 國之大用。

translations.literal: State's great use.
translations.idiomatic: They are the state's great instrument.

sentences.zh: 安寧則以別尊卑之序，有變則以濟遠近之難。

translations.literal: Peaceful calm then use separate noble-base order—when change then aid far-near difficulty.
translations.idiomatic: In peace they set rank; in crisis they bridge distances.'

sentences.zh: 昔有騏驥，一日千里，伯樂見之，昭然不惑。

translations.literal: Formerly there was Qiji—one day thousand li—Bole saw him—clearly not confused.
translations.idiomatic: The steed Qiji ran a thousand li a day; Bole knew him at once.'

sentences.zh: 近世有西河子輿，亦明相法。

translations.literal: Recent generation had West River Ziyu—also understood horse physiognomy.
translations.idiomatic: In our day Ziyu of West River understood conformation.'

sentences.zh: 子輿傳西河儀長孺，長孺傳茂陵丁君都，君群傳成紀楊子阿，臣援嘗師事子阿，受相馬骨法。

translations.literal: Ziyu transmitted West River Yi Changru—Changru transmitted Maoling Ding Jundu—Jundu transmitted Chengji Yang Zi'e—your subject Yuan once served Zi'e as teacher—received horse-bone method.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan learned bone-reading from Yang Zi'e through Ding Jundu and Yi Changru.'

sentences.zh: 考之於行事，輒有驗效。

translations.literal: Examined in deeds—always had verifying effect.
translations.idiomatic: Tested in practice it works.'

sentences.zh: 臣愚以為傳聞不如親見，視景不如察形。

translations.literal: Your subject foolishly thinks hearsay not like seeing—view shadow not like inspecting form.
translations.idiomatic: Hearsay cannot match seeing a living horse.'

sentences.zh: 今欲形之於生馬，則骨法難備具，又不可傳之於後。

translations.literal: Now wish form on living horse—then bone method hard fully prepare—also cannot transmit posterity.
translations.idiomatic: Live horses cannot preserve bone lines for posterity.'

sentences.zh: 孝武皇帝時，善相馬者東門京鑄作銅馬法獻之，有詔立馬於魯班門外，則更名魯班門曰金馬門。

translations.literal: Emperor Wu times—good horse judger East Gate Jing cast bronze horse method presented—had edict erect horse outside Lu Ban gate—then changed Lu Ban gate name Golden Horse gate.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wu's groom Dongmen Jing cast a bronze model; the Golden Horse Gate outside Lu Ban took its name from it.'

sentences.zh: 臣謹依儀氏䩭，中帛氏口齒，謝氏唇鬐，丁氏身中，備此數家骨相以為法。

translations.literal: Your subject respectfully followed Yi bridle—Zhongbo teeth—Xie lip crest—Ding body center—combined these families bone signs as law.
translations.idiomatic: He combined Yi, Zhong, Xie, and Ding methods into one standard.'

sentences.zh: 』馬高三尺五寸，圍四尺五寸，有詔置於宣德殿下，以為名馬式焉。

translations.literal: ' Horse high three chi five cun—circumference four chi five cun—had edict place Xuande hall below—thereby as famous horse model.
translations.idiomatic: The statue stood three chi five high with girth four chi five and was set below Xuande Hall as the exemplar of a fine horse.'

sentences.zh: 初，援軍還，將至，故人多迎勞之。

translations.literal: Initially Yuan army returning—about to arrive—old friends many welcomed consoled.
translations.idiomatic: As Ma Yuan neared the capital old friends came to greet him.

sentences.zh: 平陵人孟冀，名有計謀，於坐賀援。

translations.literal: Pingling man Meng Ji—famed for stratagem—at seat congratulated Yuan.
translations.idiomatic: Meng Ji of Pingling, known for counsel, offered bland congratulations.

sentences.zh: 援謂之曰：『吾望子有善言，反同眾人邪？

translations.literal: Yuan told him: 'I hoped you would offer worthy counsel—yet you speak like the common run?'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan said, 'I expected better from you than the usual flattery.'

sentences.zh: 昔伏波將軍路博德開置七郡，裁封數百戶；

translations.literal: The wave-quelling general Lu Bode once opened seven commanderies for the court but was enfeoffed with only a few hundred households;
translations.idiomatic: Look at Lu Bode: he earned seven commanderies for the Han yet held only a few hundred households in fief;

sentences.zh: 今我微勞，猥饗大縣，功薄賞厚，何以能長久乎？

translations.literal: Now I have slight merit but am given a great county; my merit is thin and my reward is thick—how can that long endure?'
translations.idiomatic: my own service is small while my fief is huge—how can such a gap last?'

sentences.zh: 先生奚用相濟？

translations.literal: Sir, in what way do you help me?'
translations.idiomatic: What use is flattery to me?'

sentences.zh: 』冀曰：『愚不及。

translations.literal: ' Ji said: 'I am too dull to match you.'
translations.idiomatic: Meng Ji said, 'I have no answer worth the name.'

sentences.zh: 』援曰：『方今匈奴、烏桓尚擾北邊，欲自請擊之。

translations.literal: ' Yuan said: 'At present the Xiongnu and Wuhuan still trouble the north; I mean to request permission to strike them myself.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan said, 'The Xiongnu and Wuhuan still harry the north—I will ask to take the field against them.'

sentences.zh: 男兒要當死於邊野，以馬革裹屍還葬耳，何能臥床上在兒女子手中邪！

translations.literal: A man should die in the border wilds and have his bones brought home wrapped in horsehide—how can he die in bed in the hands of women and children!'
translations.idiomatic: A man ought to fall on the frontier in horsehide, not finish his days coddled by women!'

sentences.zh: 』冀曰：『諒為烈士，當如此矣。』

translations.literal: ' Ji said: 'A resolute man of purpose should indeed be so.'
translations.idiomatic: Meng Ji said, 'That is the way a true man should talk.'

sentences.zh: 還月餘，會匈奴、烏桓寇扶風，援以三輔侵擾，園陵危逼，因請行，許之。

translations.literal: Returned over month—met Xiongnu, Wuhuan raid Fufeng—Yuan because Three Adjuncts disturbed—imperial tombs danger pressed—therefore requested march—permitted.
translations.idiomatic: A month later the Xiongnu and Wuhuan struck Fufeng; Ma Yuan cited the threat to the imperial tombs and won permission to march.

sentences.zh: 自九月至京師，十二月復出屯襄國。

translations.literal: Self ninth month reach capital—twelfth month again out garrison Xiangguo.
translations.idiomatic: He reached the capital in the ninth month and campaigned again from Xiangguo in the twelfth.

sentences.zh: 詔百官祖道。

translations.literal: Edict hundred officials farewell road.
translations.idiomatic: The officials saw him off at the barrier.

sentences.zh: 援謂黃門郎梁松、竇固曰：『凡人為貴，當使可賤，如卿等欲不可復賤，居高堅自持，勉思鄙言。

translations.literal: Yuan told gentlemen Liang Song and Dou Gu at the Yellow Gates: 'When ordinary men become noble they ought still to be capable of humility; men such as you may never again be low—dwelling high you must hold yourselves stern—take my blunt words to heart.'
translations.idiomatic: He warned Liang Song and Dou Gu at the Yellow Gates: 'Power should leave room for humility; once you cannot be humble again, cling fast to discipline—remember this rustic counsel.'

sentences.zh: 』松後果以貴滿致災，固亦幾不免。

translations.literal: ' Song afterward indeed by noble full brought disaster—Gu also nearly not escaped.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song later fell to pride; Dou Gu barely escaped.

sentences.zh: 明年秋，援乃將三千騎出高柳，行雁門、代郡、上谷障塞。

translations.literal: Next year autumn—Yuan thereupon led three thousand riders out Gaoliu—went Yanmen, Dai, Shanggu barrier passes.
translations.idiomatic: The next autumn Ma Yuan led three thousand horsemen from Gaoliu through the Yanmen, Dai, and Shanggu frontier posts.

sentences.zh: 烏桓候者見漢軍至，虜遂散去，援無所得而還。

translations.literal: Wuhuan scouts saw the Han army arrive and the enemy scattered; Yuan gained nothing and returned.
translations.idiomatic: The Wuhuan scouts spotted the Han advance and melted away; Ma Yuan came back empty-handed.

sentences.zh: 援嘗有疾，梁松來候之，獨拜床下，援不答。

translations.literal: Yuan was once ill; Liang Song came to inquire, bowing alone at the foot of the couch, and Yuan did not return the courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Yuan fell ill, Liang Song called; he bowed at the bedside but Ma Yuan did not bow back.

sentences.zh: 松去後，諸子問曰：『梁伯孫帝婿，貴重朝廷，公卿已下莫不憚之，大人奈何獨不為禮？

translations.literal: After Song left, the sons asked: 'Bosun of Liang is the emperor's son-in-law, exalted at court; from the highest minister down none does not fear him—why did you alone refuse him courtesy?'
translations.idiomatic: His sons asked how he could snub Liang Song, the emperor's son-in-law, feared by the whole court.

sentences.zh: 』援曰：『我乃松父友也。

translations.literal: Yuan said: 'I am a friend of Song's father.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan replied, 'I am his father's peer.'

sentences.zh: 雖貴，何得失其序乎？

translations.literal: However noble, how may one lose the proper order of age and rank?'
translations.idiomatic: What of rank? The order between elder and younger must not be broken.'

sentences.zh: 』松由是恨之。

translations.literal: Song for this resented him.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Song nursed a grudge from that day.

sentences.zh: 二十四年，武威將軍劉尚擊武陵五溪蠻夷，深入，軍沒，援因復請行。

translations.literal: Twenty-fourth year: General of Wuwei Liu Shang struck the Wuling five-stream tribes, advanced deep, and the army was lost; Yuan therefore again requested to march.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-fourth year Liu Shang's campaign against the Wuling tribes ended in annihilation; Ma Yuan again volunteered.

sentences.zh: 時年六十二，帝湣其老，未許之。

translations.literal: He was sixty-two; the emperor pitied his age and at first did not grant it.
translations.idiomatic: At sixty-two he seemed too old; Guangwu demurred.

sentences.zh: 援自請曰：『臣尚能披甲上馬。

translations.literal: Yuan self-requested: 'Your subject can still don armor and mount a horse.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan said, 'I can still arm myself and take the saddle.'

sentences.zh: 』帝令試之。

translations.literal: The emperor ordered him put to the test.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor bade him prove it.

sentences.zh: 援據鞍顧眄，以示可用。

translations.literal: Yuan gripped the saddle and looked about to show he was fit for use.
translations.idiomatic: He seized the pommel, glanced around, and showed he was still strong.

sentences.zh: 帝笑曰：『瞿鑠哉是翁也！

translations.literal: The emperor laughed: 'What a spry old fellow!'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor laughed, 'Tough as nails, this greybeard!'

sentences.zh: 』遂遣援率中郎將馬武、耿舒、劉匡、孫永等，將十二郡募士及弛刑四萬餘人征五溪。

translations.literal: Thereupon he sent Yuan leading generals of the household Ma Wu, Geng Shu, Liu Kuang, and Sun Yong with levies from twelve commanderies and over forty thousand commuted convicts to campaign against the five streams.
translations.idiomatic: He gave Ma Wu, Geng Shu, Liu Kuang, and Sun Yong, over forty thousand recruits and remitted convicts from twelve commanderies, to subdue the five streams.

sentences.zh: 援夜與送者訣，謂友人謂者杜愔曰：『吾受厚恩，年迫餘日索，常恐不得死國事。

translations.literal: Yuan at night bade farewell to those who sent him off, saying to his friend the usher Du Yin: 'I have received deep favor; the days left to me are few, and I always feared I would not die in the state's service.'
translations.idiomatic: The night before marching he told Du Yin, 'I have enjoyed great favor; I feared I might not die in service to the realm.'

sentences.zh: 今獲所願，甘心瞑目，但畏長者家兒或在左右，或與從事，殊難得調，介介獨惡是耳。

translations.literal: Now I have gained my wish and may close my eyes content—only I fear the sons of great families who may stand at my side or serve with me; they are hard to command, and I alone chafe at that.'
translations.idiomatic: Now I have my wish and can die content—save that wellborn youths on my staff are hard to manage, and that alone vexes me.'

sentences.zh: 』明年春，軍至臨鄉，遇賊攻縣，援迎擊，破之，斬獲二千餘人，皆散走入竹林中。

translations.literal: ' The next spring the army reached Linxiang, met bandits besieging a county; Yuan met them, broke them, and took over two thousand heads; all scattered into the bamboo groves.
translations.idiomatic: The next spring at Linxiang he rescued a besieged county, killed two thousand, and drove the rest into the bamboo.

sentences.zh: 初，軍次下雋，有兩道可人，從壺頭則路近而水崄，從充則塗夷而運遠，帝初以為疑。

translations.literal: Initially the army halted at Xiajun: two routes inward—by Hutou the road was short but the water dangerous; by Chong the road was level but transport long. The emperor at first was uncertain.
translations.idiomatic: Below Xiajun two routes offered themselves: Hutou was closer but the water treacherous; Chong was level but the supply line long. The emperor had hesitated between them.

sentences.zh: 及軍至，耿舒欲從充道，援以為棄日費糧，不如進壺頭，扼其喉咽，充賊自破。

translations.literal: When the army arrived, Geng Shu wished to take the Chong road; Yuan thought to waste days and grain was worse than pushing Hutou, seizing the throat, and letting the Chong bandits break of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Shu wanted the easier Chong road; Ma Yuan chose Hutou to strangle the enemy, though the water was worse.

sentences.zh: 以事上之，帝從援策。

translations.literal: He reported the matter to the throne; the emperor followed Yuan's plan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved Ma Yuan's plan.

sentences.zh: 三月，進營壺頭。

translations.literal: Third month: advanced and camped at Hutou.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month he camped at Hutou.

sentences.zh: 賊乘高守隘，水疾，船不得上。

translations.literal: The bandits held the heights and guarded the narrows; the current ran fast and boats could not mount.
translations.idiomatic: The enemy held the defiles; the rapids stopped the boats.

sentences.zh: 會暑甚，士卒多疫死，援亦中病，遂困，乃穿岸為室，以避炎氣。

translations.literal: The heat was extreme; many soldiers died of plague; Yuan also sickened and was trapped; he therefore cut the bank to make a chamber to escape the scorching air.
translations.idiomatic: Summer sickness decimated the army; Ma Yuan fell ill and hollowed the riverbank for shelter from the heat.

sentences.zh: 賊每升險鼓噪，援輒曳足以觀之，左右哀其壯意，莫不為之流涕。

translations.literal: Whenever the bandits climbed the heights beating drums, Yuan would drag himself forth to watch; those beside him pitied his heroic resolve and none could hold back tears.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever the tribesmen drummed on the cliffs he dragged himself out to look; his officers wept at his courage.

sentences.zh: 耿舒與兄好畤侯弇書曰：『前舒上書當先擊充，糧雖難運而兵馬得用，軍人數萬爭欲先奮。

translations.literal: Geng Shu wrote his brother Marquis of Haozhi Yan: 'Earlier Shu memorialized that we should strike Chong first; grain would be hard to move but the troops could fight—tens of thousands of soldiers were eager to advance first.'
translations.idiomatic: Geng Shu wrote to his brother Geng Yan that he had urged the Chong route while supplies could still move.

sentences.zh: 今壺頭竟不得進，大眾怫郁行死，誠可痛惜。

translations.literal: Now Hutou cannot be forced; the great host simmers and wastes—a bitter pity.
translations.idiomatic: Now they were stuck at Hutou while men wasted away.

sentences.zh: 前到臨鄉，賊無故自致，若夜擊之，即可殄滅。

translations.literal: Earlier at Linxiang the bandits offered themselves for nothing; had we struck by night we might have wiped them out.
translations.idiomatic: At Linxiang the enemy had been ripe for a night attack.

sentences.zh: 伏波類西域賈胡，到一處輒止，以是失利。

translations.literal: The wave-queller is like a western-region merchant—every place he stops and loses advantage.
translations.idiomatic: He mocked Ma Yuan as a peddler who halted at every halt.

sentences.zh: 今果疾疫，皆如舒言。

translations.literal: Now plague has indeed come, all as Shu predicted.
translations.idiomatic: Now sickness proved Shu right.

sentences.zh: 』弇得書，奏之。

translations.literal: ' Yan received the letter and memorialized it.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan forwarded the letter to the throne.

sentences.zh: 帝乃使虎賁中郎將梁松乘驛責問援，因代監軍。

translations.literal: The emperor then ordered Colonel of Gentlemen Liang Song by post-station to reprimand Yuan and replace him as army supervisor.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu sent Liang Song by relay to rebuke Ma Yuan and take over supervision.

sentences.zh: 會援病卒，松宿懷不平，遂因事陷之。

translations.literal: When Yuan died of illness, Song long harbored resentment and therefore used the affair to frame him.
translations.idiomatic: When Ma Yuan died Liang Song seized the chance to slander him.

sentences.zh: 帝大怒，追收援新息侯印綬。

translations.literal: The emperor greatly angry pursued and seized Yuan's marquis of Xixin seals.
translations.idiomatic: In wrath the emperor stripped the marquisate of Xixin.

sentences.zh: 初，兄子嚴、敦並喜譏議，而通輕俠客。

translations.literal: Initially his nephews Yan and Dun both loved satire and debate and freely trusted wandering swordsmen.
translations.idiomatic: His nephews Ma Yan and Ma Dun loved gossip and mixed with bravos.

sentences.zh: 援前在交阯，還書誡之曰：『吾欲汝曹聞人過失，如聞父母之名，耳可得聞，口不可得言也。

translations.literal: Yuan earlier in Jiaozhi sent a letter warning them: 'I wish you when hearing others' faults to be as when hearing your parents' names—ears may hear but mouth must not speak.'
translations.idiomatic: From Jiaozhi he had written: treat others' faults like parents' personal names—heard but not spoken.

sentences.zh: 好論議人長短，妄是非正法，此吾所大惡也，寧死不願聞子孫有此行也。

translations.literal: Loving to debate people's strengths and weaknesses and recklessly judge right and wrong is what I most hate; I would rather die than hear my grandchildren behave so.
translations.idiomatic: He loathed idle judgment of others and forbade his kin to indulge it.

sentences.zh: 汝曹知吾惡之甚矣，所以復言者，施衿結䙰，申父母之戒，欲使汝曹不忘之耳。

translations.literal: You know how I detest this; I speak again only as when tying sash and hood—repeating parents' warnings—wishing you not to forget.
translations.idiomatic: He repeated the lesson like a parent's parting charge.

sentences.zh: 龍伯高敦厚周慎，口無擇言，謙約節儉，廉公有威，吾愛之重之，願汝曹效之。

translations.literal: Long Bogao is steady, thorough, and cautious, speaks no careless word, is humble and frugal, honest and awe-inspiring—I love and esteem him; I wish you would imitate him.
translations.idiomatic: He praised Long Shu for sober virtue and urged them to emulate him.

sentences.zh: 杜季良豪俠好義，憂人之憂，樂人之樂，清濁無所失，父喪致客，數郡畢至，吾愛之重之，不願汝曹效也。

translations.literal: Du Jiliang is chivalrous and fond of duty; he shares others' sorrow and joy; high and low miss none; at his father's funeral guests came from several commanderies—I love and esteem him but do not wish you to imitate him.
translations.idiomatic: Du Baozhong was generous to a fault; admire him but do not copy him.

sentences.zh: 效伯高不得，猶為謹敕之士，所謂刻鵠不成尚類鶩者也。

translations.literal: If you imitate Bogao and fall short, you remain a careful gentleman—the saying about carving a swan and still getting a duck.
translations.idiomatic: Missing Bogao's mark still leaves you a sober man—like carving a swan and getting a duck.

sentences.zh: 效季良不得，陷為天下輕薄子，所謂畫虎不成反類狗者也。

translations.literal: If you imitate Jiliang and fall short, you sink to become a shallow rake—the saying about painting a tiger and getting a dog.
translations.idiomatic: Missing Jiliang's mark makes you a swaggering fool—the tiger that becomes a dog.

sentences.zh: 迄今季良尚未可知，郡將下車輒切齒，州郡以為言，吾常為寒心，是以不願子孫效也。

translations.literal: To this day Jiliang's outcome is uncertain; commandery generals grit teeth when he arrives and provinces complain—I often shiver for him; therefore I do not wish descendants to imitate him.
translations.idiomatic: Officials still curse Du Baozhong; Ma Yuan trembled for him and forbade imitation.

sentences.zh: 』季良名保，京兆人，時為越騎司馬。

translations.literal: ' Jiliang's given name was Bao; he was a man of Jingzhao and then served as colonel of agile cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: Du Baozhong's name was Bao of Jingzhao; he was a colonel of agile cavalry.

sentences.zh: 保仇人上書，訟保『為行浮薄，亂群惑眾，伏波將軍萬里還書以誡兄子，而梁松、竇固以之交結，將扇其輕偽，敗亂諸夏。

translations.literal: Bao's enemy submitted a memorial accusing Bao of 'conduct shallow and dissolute, confusing crowds and deluding the multitude; the wave-queller wrote from ten thousand li to warn his nephews, yet Liang Song and Dou Gu fraternize with him and will fan his levity and corrupt the central states.'
translations.idiomatic: A foe impeached Du Bao; the memorial cited Ma Yuan's letter and attacked Liang Song and Dou Gu for befriending him.

sentences.zh: 』書奏，帝召責松、固，以訟書及援誡書示之，松、固叩頭流血，而得不罪。

translations.literal: ' When the memorial arrived the emperor summoned Song and Gu to rebuke them, showed the accusation and Yuan's warning letter; Song and Gu knocked heads until blood flowed and escaped punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu confronted Liang Song and Dou Gu with both letters; they kowtowed in terror.

sentences.zh: 詔免保官。

translations.literal: An edict dismissed Bao from office.
translations.idiomatic: Du Bao lost his post.

sentences.zh: 伯高名述，亦京兆人，為山都長，由此擢拜零陵太守。

translations.literal: Bogao's name was Shu, also of Jingzhao; he was chief of Shandu and was therefore promoted governor of Lingling.
translations.idiomatic: Long Shu rose from magistrate of Shandu to governor of Lingling.

sentences.zh: 初，援在交阯，常餌薏苡實，用能輕身省欲，以勝瘴氣。

translations.literal: Initially Yuan in Jiaozhi regularly ate coix seed to lighten the body and curb desire and thereby overcome miasma.
translations.idiomatic: In Jiaozhi Ma Yuan ate coix to resist miasma.

sentences.zh: 南方薏苡實大，援欲以為種，軍還，載之一車。

translations.literal: The southern coix fruit was large; Yuan wished to take seed; when the army returned he loaded one cart.
translations.idiomatic: He brought back a cart of large southern coix for seed.

sentences.zh: 時人以為南士珍怪，權貴皆望之。

translations.literal: People of the time thought them southern curiosities; the powerful all coveted them.
translations.idiomatic: Rumor mistook the grain for treasure; grandees envied the load.

sentences.zh: 援時方有寵，故莫以聞。

translations.literal: Yuan was then in favor, so none reported it.
translations.idiomatic: While Ma Yuan lived in favor no one dared accuse him.

sentences.zh: 及卒後，有上書譖之者，以為前所載還，皆明珠文犀。

translations.literal: After he died some memorialized slandering him, claiming what he had shipped were luminous pearls and patterned rhinoceros horn.
translations.idiomatic: After his death malice claimed his carts held pearls and rhino horn.

sentences.zh: 馬武與於陵侯侯昱等皆以章言其狀，帝益怒。

translations.literal: Ma Wu and Marquis of Yuling Hou Yu and others all cited the memorial describing the situation; the emperor grew angrier.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Wu and Hou Yu repeated the charges and enraged the emperor further.

sentences.zh: 援妻孥惶懼，不敢以喪還舊塋，裁買城西數畝地槁葬而已。

translations.literal: Yuan's wife and children trembled in fear and dared not bring the coffin to the old tomb; they barely bought several mu west of the city for a rough burial.
translations.idiomatic: His family dared not bury him at home and laid him in a few mu west of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 賓客故人莫敢吊會。

translations.literal: Retainers and old friends none dared mourn or assemble.
translations.idiomatic: Friends feared to attend the funeral.

sentences.zh: 嚴與援妻子草索相連，詣闕請罪。

translations.literal: Yan with Yuan's wife and children tied themselves with straw rope and went to the palace gate to beg punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yan led the widow and children bound in straw to beg forgiveness at the gate.

sentences.zh: 帝乃出松書以示之，方知所坐，上書訴冤，前後六上，辭甚哀切，然後得葬。

translations.literal: The emperor then produced Song's letter to show them; only then did they know the charge; they memorialized injustice six times in moving words before burial was allowed.
translations.idiomatic: Only when Guangwu showed Liang Song's letter could they plead; after six appeals they won proper burial.

sentences.zh: 又前雲陽令同郡朱勃詣闕上書曰：

translations.literal: Further, former magistrate of Yunyang Zhu Bo of the same commandery went to the gate submitting a memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo of Yunyang then submitted this memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞王德聖政，不忘人之功，采其一美，不求備於眾。

translations.literal: Your subject hears that kingly virtue and sage government do not forget men's merit—take one excellence and do not demand completeness from all.
translations.idiomatic: True kings remember single virtues and do not demand perfection.

sentences.zh: 故高祖赦蒯通而以王禮葬田橫，大臣曠然，咸不自疑。

translations.literal: Therefore Gaozu pardoned Kuai Tong and buried Tian Heng with royal rites; great ministers were easy in mind and none doubted themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu pardoned Kuai Tong and honored Tian Heng; ministers trusted their sovereign.

sentences.zh: 夫大將在外，讒言在內，微過輒記，大功不計，誠為國之所慎也。

translations.literal: When great generals are abroad and slander flourishes at court, small faults are recorded and great deeds ignored—that is what a state must guard against.
translations.idiomatic: Courts that tally petty faults abroad invite disaster.

sentences.zh: 故章邯畏口而奔楚，燕將據聊而不下。

translations.literal: Therefore Zhang Han fled to Chu for fear of slander; the Yan general held Liaocheng and could not take it.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Han fled calumny; the Yan siege of Liaocheng stalled.

sentences.zh: 豈其甘心末規哉，悼巧言之傷類也。

translations.literal: Was it that they wished petty ends? They mourned how clever words wound their kind.
translations.idiomatic: They feared malicious counsel, not cowardice.

sentences.zh: 竊見故伏波將軍新息侯馬援，拔自西州，欽慕聖義，間關險難，觸冒萬死，孤立群貴之間，傍無一言之佐，馳深淵，入虎口，豈顧計哉！

translations.literal: Your subject has observed the late wave-queller Marquis of Xixin Ma Yuan: raised from the western provinces, admiring sagely righteousness, threading peril and hardship, braving ten thousand deaths, standing alone among the noble—without one ally—charging deep pools and tiger mouths—did he count the cost!
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo praised Ma Yuan's western service and perilous loyalty.

sentences.zh: 寧自知當要七郡之使，僥封侯之福邪？

translations.literal: Could he have known he would meet envoys of seven commanderies and barely win a marquisate?'
translations.idiomatic: Could he have hoped for seven commanderies and a noble title?'

sentences.zh: 八年，車駕西討隗囂，國計狐疑，眾營未集，援建宜進之策，卒破西州。

translations.literal: Eighth year: imperial carriage west campaigned Wei Xiao; state policy wavered and camps were not massed; Yuan proposed advance—finally broke the western provinces.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year his counsel broke Wei Xiao.

sentences.zh: 及吳漢下隴，冀路斷隔，惟獨狄道為國堅守，士民饑困，寄命漏刻。

translations.literal: When Wu Han descended Long, the Ji road was cut; only Didao held for the state—soldiers and people starved, lives hung by moments.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Han took Long, Didao alone held out on the brink of starvation.

sentences.zh: 援奉詔西使，鎮慰邊眾，乃招集豪傑，曉誘羌戎，謀如湧泉，勢如轉規，遂救倒縣之急，存幾亡之城，兵全師進，因糧敵人，隴、冀略平，而獨守空郡，兵動有功，師進輒克。

translations.literal: Yuan received edict to go west, pacify the border peoples, gather heroes, instruct Qiang and Rong—plans welled like springs, momentum turned like a wheel—thus relieved the hanging peril, preserved nearly lost cities, preserved the army advancing, fed off the enemy—Long and Ji were nearly pacified though he alone defended empty commanderies; wherever troops moved they succeeded and wherever they advanced they conquered.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan's western mission rallied allies, fed the army from the foe, and saved Didao.

sentences.zh: 銖鋤先零，緣入山谷，猛怒力戰，飛矢貫脛。

translations.literal: He rooted out the Xianling, traced them into the valleys, fought furiously; a flying arrow pierced his shin.
translations.idiomatic: He drove the Xianling into the hills and took an arrow in the leg.

sentences.zh: 又出征交阯，土多瘴氣，援與妻子生訣，無悔吝之心，遂斬滅徵側，克平一州，間復南討，立陷臨鄉，師已有業，未竟而死，吏士雖疫，援不獨存。

translations.literal: Again he campaigned Jiaozhi where soil bore heavy miasma; Yuan bade wife and children farewell without regret, exterminated Zheng Ce and pacified a province; again he marched south, seized Linxiang—success was at hand when he died before finishing; though officers and men sickened, Yuan did not alone survive.
translations.idiomatic: He risked miasma in the south, executed the Zheng sisters, then died at Hutou while his men still fell to plague.

sentences.zh: 夫戰或以久而立功，或以速而致敗，深入未必為得，不進未必為非。

translations.literal: Battle sometimes wins by length of time, sometimes loses by haste; deep advance is not always gain, standing still not always error.
translations.idiomatic: Campaigns turn on timing; neither delay nor dash guarantees success.

sentences.zh: 人情豈樂久屯絕地，不生歸哉！

translations.literal: How could human hearts delight in long encampment on deadly ground with no hope of return!
translations.idiomatic: Who would willingly rot in a killing field?

sentences.zh: 惟援得事朝廷二十二年，北出塞漠，南度江海，觸冒害氣，僵死軍事，名滅爵絕，國土不傳。

translations.literal: Yet Yuan served the court twenty-two years—north beyond the passes and desert, south across river and sea—breathing poison air, dying in military affairs—name extinguished, noble rank ended, fief not transmitted.
translations.idiomatic: Twenty-two years he served north and south and died in harness, stripped of honor.

sentences.zh: 海內不知其過，眾庶未聞其毀，卒遇三夫之言，橫被誣罔之讒，家屬杜門，葬不歸墓，怨隙並興，宗親怖栗。

translations.literal: Within the seas none knows his fault; the multitude has not heard his shame; suddenly encountering three men's words he was smeared with false slander—family barred doors, burial not at ancestral tomb—resentment welled together and kin trembled.
translations.idiomatic: Slander alone ruined him; kin sealed their doors in terror.

sentences.zh: 死者不能自列，生者莫為之訟，臣竊傷之。

translations.literal: The dead cannot speak for themselves; the living none pleaded for—your subject privately grieves.
translations.idiomatic: The dead cannot defend themselves; Zhu Bo grieved for Ma Yuan.

sentences.zh: 夫明主醲於用賞，約於用刑。

translations.literal: A clear lord is rich in reward and spare in punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Wise rulers reward generously and punish sparingly.

sentences.zh: 高祖嘗與陳平金四萬斤以間楚軍，不問出入所為，豈復疑以錢谷間哉？

translations.literal: Gaozu once gave Chen Ping forty thousand jin of gold to sow discord in Chu and asked no accounting—would he suspect coin and grain?'
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu trusted Chen Ping with gold—would he nitpick accounts?'

sentences.zh: 夫操孔父之忠而不能自免於讒，此鄒陽之所悲也。

translations.literal: Though one wield Confucius's loyalty one cannot escape slander—that is what Zou Yang mourned.
translations.idiomatic: Even Confucius's loyalty did not foil slander—Zou Yang wept for it.

sentences.zh: 《詩》云：『取彼讒人，投畀豺虎。

translations.literal: The Odes say: 'Take those slanderers and cast them to wolves and tigers.'
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Odes says: cast slanderers to wolves.'

sentences.zh: 豺虎不食，投畀有北。

translations.literal: If wolves and tigers will not eat them, cast them to the north.
translations.idiomatic: If wolves refuse them, banish them north.'

sentences.zh: 有北不受，投畀有昊。

translations.literal: If the north will not receive them, cast them to heaven.
translations.idiomatic: If the north rejects them, give them to Heaven.'

sentences.zh: 』此言欲令上天而平其惡。
