translations.literal: ' This speech wishes heaven to level their evil.
translations.idiomatic: The verse asks Heaven to judge malice.'

sentences.zh: 惟陛下留思豎儒之言，無使功臣懷恨黃泉。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty ponder the words of this rustic scholar and not let a merit minister harbor resentment in the yellow springs.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo begged Guangwu not to let Ma Yuan hate him in the grave.

sentences.zh: 臣聞《春秋》之義，罪以功除；

translations.literal: Your subject hears the Spring and Autumn meaning: guilt may be removed by merit;
translations.idiomatic: The Spring and Autumn forgives guilt for merit;

sentences.zh: 聖王之祀，臣有五義。

translations.literal: in sacrifices to sage kings there are five kinds of service.
translations.idiomatic: the sage king's temple honors five kinds of service.

sentences.zh: 若援，所謂以死勤事者也。

translations.literal: As for Yuan—this is what is called dying in diligent service.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan died in diligent service.

sentences.zh: 願下公卿平援功罪，宜絕宜續，以厭海內之望。

translations.literal: Wish that you send down to dukes and ministers to weigh Yuan's merit and guilt—whether to cut off or continue—and thereby satisfy expectation within the seas.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo asked the court to retry Ma Yuan's case for the empire's sake.

sentences.zh: 臣年已六十，常伏田里，竊感欒布哭彭越之義，冒陳悲憤，戰栗闕庭。

translations.literal: Your subject is already sixty, often hiding in the fields; privately moved by Luan Bu weeping for Peng Yue's justice, I risk presenting grief and anger, trembling in the palace court.
translations.idiomatic: At sixty Zhu Bo risked all, like Luan Bu mourning Peng Yue.

sentences.zh: 書奏，報，歸田里。

translations.literal: Memorial submitted; reply came; return to fields.
translations.idiomatic: The court answered and sent Zhu Bo home.

sentences.zh: 勃字叔陽，年十二能誦《詩》、《書》。

translations.literal: Bo's style was Shuyang; at twelve he could recite the Odes and Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo, style Shuyang, knew the classics at twelve.

sentences.zh: 常候援兄況。

translations.literal: He often attended Yuan's elder brother Kuang.
translations.idiomatic: He studied under Ma Kuang.

sentences.zh: 勃衣方領，能矩步。

translations.literal: Bo wore square collar and could walk measured steps.
translations.idiomatic: He wore scholar's square collar and walked with measured stride.

sentences.zh: 辭言嫻雅，援裁知書，見之自失。

translations.literal: His speech was refined; Yuan barely knew letters and seeing him felt himself lacking.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan, barely literate, felt crude beside him.

sentences.zh: 況知其意，乃自酌酒慰援曰：『朱勃小器速成，智盡此耳，卒當從汝稟學，勿畏也。

translations.literal: Kuang knew his mind and himself poured wine consoling Yuan: 'Zhu Bo is a small vessel quick to fill—his wit ends here; in the end he will come study with you—do not fear.'
translations.idiomatic: Ma Kuang reassured Ma Yuan that Zhu Bo's polish was shallow youth.

sentences.zh: 』朱勃未二十，右扶風請試守渭城宰，及援為將軍，封侯，而勃位不過縣令。

translations.literal: ' Zhu Bo before twenty—Right Fufeng requested him acting magistrate of Weicheng; when Yuan became general and was enfeoffed marquis, Bo's rank did not exceed county magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo peaked as county magistrate while Ma Yuan rose to marquis.

sentences.zh: 援後雖貴，常待以舊恩而卑侮之，勃愈身自親，及援遇讒，唯勃能終焉。

translations.literal: Though Yuan later was noble he always treated him with old kindness though humble and insulting him; Bo became ever more intimate; when Yuan met slander only Bo saw him through.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan still humbled Zhu Bo; only Zhu Bo stood by him in disgrace.

sentences.zh: 肅宗即位，追賜勃子穀二千斛。

translations.literal: When Emperor Zhang ascended he posthumously granted Bo's son two thousand hu of grain.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang gave Zhu Bo's son two thousand hu.

sentences.zh: 永平初，援女立為皇后，顯宗圖畫建武中名臣、列將於雲臺，以椒房故，獨不及援。

translations.literal: Early Yongping: Yuan's daughter was established empress; Emperor Ming painted Jianwu era famous ministers and commanders on Cloud Terrace—because of the empress's chamber alone Yuan was omitted.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming's Cloud Terrace portrait gallery omitted Ma Yuan because of the connection.

sentences.zh: 東平王蒼觀圖，言於帝曰：『何故不畫伏波將軍像？

translations.literal: Prince of Dongping Cang viewed the paintings and said to the emperor: 'Why is no portrait of the wave-queller painted?'
translations.idiomatic: Prince Cang asked why Ma Yuan was missing.

sentences.zh: 』帝笑而不言。

translations.literal: ' The emperor smiled and did not speak.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor smiled and said nothing.

sentences.zh: 至十七年，援夫人卒，乃更修封樹，起祠堂。

translations.literal: Not until the seventeenth year when Yuan's lady died did they repair the mound and trees and raise a shrine.
translations.idiomatic: Only in the seventeenth year, after his wife died, was his tomb restored and a shrine raised.

sentences.zh: 建初三年，肅宗使五官中郎將持節追策，謚援曰忠成侯。

translations.literal: Third year of Jianchu: Emperor Zhang sent the general of the household for all purposes with staff to posthumously advance Ma Yuan's title—posthumous name Marquis Zhongcheng.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 3 Emperor Zhang posthumously titled Ma Yuan Marquis Zhongcheng.

sentences.zh: 四子：廖、防、光、客卿。

translations.literal: Four sons: Liao, Fang, Guang, and Keqing.
translations.idiomatic: He left four sons: Ma Liao, Ma Fang, Ma Guang, and Ma Keqing.

sentences.zh: 客卿幼而歧嶷，年六歲，能應接諸公，專對賓客。

translations.literal: Keqing was precocious from childhood; at six he could receive dukes and answer guests on his own.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Keqing was a prodigy who at six could receive grandees and hold his own with visitors.

sentences.zh: 嘗有死罪亡命者來過，客卿逃匿不令人知。

translations.literal: Once a man under sentence of death fled and came by; Keqing hid him and let no one know.
translations.idiomatic: When a fugitive facing execution sought refuge, the boy hid him from sight.

sentences.zh: 外若訥而內沈敏。

translations.literal: Outwardly he seemed slow; inwardly he was deep and keen.
translations.idiomatic: He seemed dull in speech but was acute beneath.

sentences.zh: 援甚奇之，以為將相器，故以客卿字焉。

translations.literal: Yuan greatly marveled at him, thinking him ministerial timber, and therefore gave him the courtesy name Keqing ('guest minister').
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan saw ministerial gifts in him and styled him Keqing.

sentences.zh: 援卒後，客卿亦夭沒。

translations.literal: After Yuan died, Keqing also died young.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Keqing died in childhood after his father.

sentences.zh: 論曰：馬援騰聲三輔，遨遊二帝，及定節立謀，以幹時主，將懷負鼎之願，蓋為千載之遇焉。

translations.literal: Discourse says: Ma Yuan made his name in the Three Adjuncts and shuttled between rival emperors; once he fixed his integrity and laid plans to serve the ruler of the age, he harbored the wish to bear the sacred cauldron—surely an encounter once in a thousand years.
translations.idiomatic: The historian calls Ma Yuan's rise through loyalty to rival courts and service to Guangwu a once-in-an-age alignment with fate.

sentences.zh: 然其戒人之禍，智矣，而不能自免於讒隙。

translations.literal: Yet though wise enough to warn others against calamity, he could not himself escape the cracks of slander.
translations.idiomatic: He lectured others on peril yet fell to gossip himself.

sentences.zh: 豈功名之際，理固然乎？

translations.literal: At the summit of merit and fame—is that simply how principle works?
translations.idiomatic: Is fame's summit always stalked by envy?

sentences.zh: 夫利不在身，以之謀事則智；

translations.literal: When profit does not lie on one's person, one plans affairs with clarity;
translations.idiomatic: Detached from self-interest a man plans wisely;

sentences.zh: 慮不私己，以之斷義必厲。

translations.literal: when anxiety does not serve private ends, judgment of duty must be stern.
translations.idiomatic: when duty is not selfish his verdict on right is severe.

sentences.zh: 誠能回觀物之智而為反身之察，若施之於人則能恕，自鑒其情亦明矣。

translations.literal: Could one turn the sharp eye one trains on things inward upon oneself—such insight applied to others yields forgiveness, and turned on one's own case lights all corners.
translations.idiomatic: Turn the clarity used on others toward yourself and you forgive easily and see your own heart plain.

sentences.zh: 廖字敬平，少以父任為郎。

translations.literal: Liao, courtesy name Jingping, in youth became gentleman through his father's privilege.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Liao, style Jingping, entered as gentleman by yin privilege.

sentences.zh: 明德皇后既立，拜廖為羽林左監、虎賁中郎將。

translations.literal: After Empress Mingde was established he was appointed colonel of the Forest of Plumes on the left and colonel of the Gentlemen of the Household for All Purposes.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Ma took the throne he became colonel of the Forest of Plumes and colonel of the imperial guard.

sentences.zh: 顯宗崩，受遺詔典掌門禁，遂代趙熹為衛尉，肅宗甚尊重之。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ming died he received the testament edict managing palace gates, replaced Zhao Xi as Commandant of the Guards, and Emperor Zhang honored him deeply.
translations.idiomatic: He held the testament commission at Emperor Ming's death, succeeded Zhao Xi as commandant of the guards, and won Emperor Zhang's trust.

sentences.zh: 時，皇太后躬履節儉，事從簡約，廖慮美業難終，上疏長樂宮以勸成德政，曰：

translations.literal: At that time the empress dowager personally practiced frugality and kept affairs plain; Liao feared the fine enterprise might not last and submitted a memorial to Changle Palace urging completion of virtuous government, saying:
translations.idiomatic: The dowager lived modestly; Ma Liao wrote to Changle Palace urging her to finish the good work she had begun:

sentences.zh: 臣案前世詔令，以百姓不足，起於世尚奢靡，故元帝罷服官，成帝御浣衣，哀帝去樂府。

translations.literal: Your subject reviews former ages' edicts: because the hundred surnames lacked enough, the age prized luxury—therefore Emperor Yuan abolished the robe office, Emperor Cheng wore washed garments, Emperor Ai dismissed the Music Bureau.
translations.idiomatic: Past edicts blamed popular want on fashion—Yuan, Cheng, and Ai each cut court excess.

sentences.zh: 然而侈費不息，至於衰亂者，百姓從行不從言也。

translations.literal: Yet wasteful spending did not cease; decay and chaos followed because the people followed deeds, not words.
translations.idiomatic: Yet waste continued because the people mimic action, not slogans.

sentences.zh: 夫改政移風，必有其本。

translations.literal: To change government and shift custom there must be a root.
translations.idiomatic: Reform needs a foundation.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：『吳王好劍客，百姓多創瘢；

translations.literal: Tradition says: 'The king of Wu loved swordsmen; the people bore many scars;
translations.idiomatic: The king of Wu loved swordsmen and the people scarred;

sentences.zh: 楚王好細腰，宮中多餓死』。

translations.literal: the king of Chu loved slender waists; many in the palace starved.'
translations.idiomatic: the king of Chu loved slim waists and palace girls starved.'

sentences.zh: 長安語曰：『城中好高髻，四方高一尺；

translations.literal: A Chang'an rhyme runs: 'When the capital favors high chignons, the four directions raise them a foot;
translations.idiomatic: 'Tall coiffures in the capital mean tall coiffures everywhere;

sentences.zh: 城中好廣眉，四方且半額；

translations.literal: when the capital favors broad brows, the four directions wear half the forehead;
translations.idiomatic: broad brows in court mean broad brows in the land;

sentences.zh: 城中好大袖，四方全匹帛。

translations.literal: when the capital favors wide sleeves, the four directions use whole bolts of silk.'
translations.idiomatic: wide sleeves at court exhaust whole bolts of silk.'

sentences.zh: 』斯言如戲，有切事實。

translations.literal: ' That speech sounds playful yet bites the truth.
translations.idiomatic: The verse sounds light but tells true.

sentences.zh: 前下制度未幾，後稍不行。

translations.literal: Earlier decrees had barely been issued before they slipped from practice.
translations.idiomatic: New rules seldom lasted.

sentences.zh: 雖或吏不奉法，良由慢起京師。

translations.literal: Though sometimes clerks failed the law, the fault truly began in slackness at the capital.
translations.idiomatic: When officials stray, the capital led them astray.

sentences.zh: 今陛下躬服厚繒，斥去華飾，素簡所安，發自聖性。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty personally wears heavy silk yet casts off ornament; plain simplicity is your native bent.
translations.idiomatic: You wear plain thick silk by nature, not show.

sentences.zh: 此誠上合天心，下順民望，浩大之福，莫尚於此。

translations.literal: This truly matches Heaven above and the people below; no blessing is greater.
translations.idiomatic: That harmony with Heaven and the people is the greatest blessing.

sentences.zh: 陛下既已得之自然，猶宜加以勉勖，法太宗之隆德，戒成、哀之不終。

translations.literal: Since Your Majesty has grasped this naturally, you should still press onward—model Taizong's lofty virtue and guard against Cheng and Ai who did not finish.
translations.idiomatic: Hold fast: emulate Ming Emperor's thrift and avoid Cheng and Ai's relapse.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：『不恒其德，或承之羞。

translations.literal: The Changes says: 'If he does not constant his virtue, he may suffer shame.'
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Changes warns: wavering virtue brings shame.'

sentences.zh: 』誠令斯事一竟，則四海誦德，聲董天地，神明可通，金石可勒，而況於行仁心乎，況於行令乎！

translations.literal: ' If this policy runs its course the four seas will chant your virtue, your fame will fill heaven and earth, spirits will answer, metal and stone can be carved—how much more a humane heart or lawful command!
translations.idiomatic: Finish this course and the realm will sing your praise—spirit and stone will know your mind.'

sentences.zh: 願置章坐側，以當瞽人夜誦之音。

translations.literal: I beg you keep this memorial at your seat like a blind man's night lesson.
translations.idiomatic: Keep these words beside your couch like a nightly lesson.

sentences.zh: 太后深納之。

translations.literal: The empress dowager deeply accepted it.
translations.idiomatic: The dowager embraced his counsel.

sentences.zh: 朝廷大議。

translations.literal: On great policy debates at court
translations.idiomatic: When the court deliberated major affairs

sentences.zh: 輒以詢訪。

translations.literal: she routinely consulted him for counsel.
translations.idiomatic: the empress dowager routinely sought Ma Liao's counsel.

sentences.zh: 廖性質誠畏慎，不愛權勢聲名，盡心納忠，不屑毀譽。

translations.literal: Liao's nature was sincere and cautious; he did not crave power or fame; he devoted himself loyally and scorned praise and blame.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Liao was modest, avoided ostentation, served faithfully, and cared little for reputation.

sentences.zh: 有司連據舊典，奏封廖等，累讓不得已，建初四年，遂受封為順陽侯，以特進就第。

translations.literal: Authorities repeatedly cited precedent and memorialized enfeoffment for Liao and others; he declined until he could not refuse—in the fourth year of Jianchu he accepted enfeoffment as marquis of Shunyang with rank Specially Advanced at his mansion.
translations.idiomatic: Court officials pressed a fief until he accepted the marquisate of Shunyang in Jianchu 4 as Specially Advanced.

sentences.zh: 每有賞賜，輒辭讓不敢當，京師以是稱之。

translations.literal: Whenever rewarded he declined and dared not accept; the capital praised him for it.
translations.idiomatic: He refused gifts and Luoyang admired him.

sentences.zh: 子豫，為步兵校尉。

translations.literal: Son Yu served as colonel of foot soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: His son Ma Yu became colonel of foot soldiers.

sentences.zh: 太后崩後，馬氏失勢，廖性寬緩，不能教勒子孫，豫遂投書怨誹。

translations.literal: After the dowager died the Ma clan lost influence; Liao was lax and could not discipline his descendants; Yu then posted letters of resentment and slander.
translations.idiomatic: When the dowager died Ma Yu vented slander in letters; Ma Liao had been too mild to restrain him.

sentences.zh: 又防、光奢侈，好樹黨與。

translations.literal: Moreover Fang and Guang lived extravagantly and loved building factions.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Fang and Ma Guang lived lavishly and cultivated factions.

sentences.zh: 八年，有司奏免豫，遣廖、防、光就封。

translations.literal: In the eighth year authorities memorialized Yu's dismissal and ordered Liao, Fang, and Guang to their fiefs.
translations.idiomatic: In year 8 officials cashiered Ma Yu and sent the brothers to their estates.

sentences.zh: 豫隨廖歸國，考擊物故。

translations.literal: Yu followed Liao back to his kingdom and was beaten to death in interrogation.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yu returned with his father and died under the rod.

sentences.zh: 後詔還廖京師。

translations.literal: Later an edict recalled Liao to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: An edict later recalled Ma Liao to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 永元四年，卒。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yongyuan he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Yongyuan 4.

sentences.zh: 和帝以廖先帝之舅，厚加赗賻，使者弔祭，王主會喪，謚曰安侯。

translations.literal: Emperor He, because Liao was a maternal uncle of the late emperor, lavished funeral gifts, sent envoys to mourn, had princesses attend the rites, and gave the posthumous title Marquis An.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor He buried him as uncle of the late emperor with rich gifts and the title Marquis An.

sentences.zh: 子遵嗣，徙封程鄉侯。

translations.literal: Son Zun succeeded and was transferred enfeoffment to marquis of Chengxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Zun inherited and moved the fief to Chengxiang.

sentences.zh: 遵卒，無子，國除。

translations.literal: Zun died without sons; the marquisate was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Zun died heirless and the fief lapsed.

sentences.zh: 元初三年，鄧太后詔封廖孫度為潁陽侯。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yuanchu Empress Deng issued an edict enfeoffing Liao's grandson Du as marquis of Yingyang.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanchu 3 Empress Deng revived the line with Ma Du as marquis of Yingyang.

sentences.zh: 廖弟防

translations.literal: Liao's younger brother: Fang.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Fang was the younger brother of Ma Liao.

sentences.zh: 防字江平，永平十二年，與弟光俱為黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: Fang, courtesy name Jiangping—in the twelfth year of Yongping he and his brother Guang both served as gentlemen at the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Fang, style Jiangping, and Ma Guang were gentlemen at the Yellow Gates in Yongping 12.

sentences.zh: 肅宗即位，拜防中郎將，稍遷城門校尉。

translations.literal: When Emperor Zhang ascended he appointed Fang general of the household and gradually promoted him to colonel of the City Gates.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang made him general of the household and then colonel of the gates.

sentences.zh: 建初二年，金城、隴西保塞羌皆反，拜防行車騎將軍事，以長水校尉耿恭副，將北軍五校兵及諸郡積射士三萬人擊之。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianchu the pass-guard Qiang of Jincheng and Longxi all rebelled; Fang was appointed acting chariots-and-cavalry general with Colonel of the Long River Geng Gong as deputy, leading three tithes of the northern army's five colonels and marksmen levied from the commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 2 the border Qiang rebelled; Ma Fang led thirty thousand with Geng Gong as deputy.

sentences.zh: 軍到冀，而羌豪布橋等圍南部都尉於臨洮。

translations.literal: When the army reached Ji, Qiang chiefs such as Buqiao besieged the southern commandant at Lintao.
translations.idiomatic: At Ji, Buqiao besieged the southern commandant at Lintao.

sentences.zh: 防欲救之，臨洮道險，車騎不得方駕，防乃別使兩司馬將數百騎，分為前後軍，去臨洮十餘里為大營，多樹幡幟，揚言大兵旦當進。

translations.literal: Fang wished to relieve them but the Lintao road was perilous and chariots could not drive abreast; he therefore sent two majors with several hundred horsemen as van and rear, encamped over ten li from Lintao, planted many banners, and claimed a great host would advance at dawn.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Fang feigned a large camp with banners while sending light troops to lift the siege.

sentences.zh: 羌候見之，馳還言漢兵盛不可當。

translations.literal: Qiang scouts saw it and galloped back reporting the Han host too strong to withstand.
translations.idiomatic: Qiang scouts overestimated his numbers.

sentences.zh: 明旦遂鼓噪而前，羌虜驚走，因追擊破之。

translations.literal: At next dawn he drummed forward; the Qiang fled in alarm and he pursued and broke them.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn his drums scattered the tribesmen.

sentences.zh: 斬首虜四千餘人，遂解臨洮圍。

translations.literal: Over four thousand heads were taken and the siege of Lintao was lifted.
translations.idiomatic: He took four thousand heads and freed Lintao.

sentences.zh: 防開以恩信，燒當種皆降，唯布橋等二萬餘人在臨洮西南望曲谷。

translations.literal: Fang opened ranks with kindness and trust; the Shao Dang band all surrendered; only Buqiao and over twenty thousand men remained southwest of Lintao in Wangqu Valley.
translations.idiomatic: The Shao Dang Qiang submitted; Buqiao held out in Wangqu Valley.

sentences.zh: 十二月，羌又敗耿恭司馬及隴西長史於和羅谷，死者數百人。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month the Qiang again defeated Geng Gong's major and the Longxi chief clerk at Heluo Valley—several hundred dead.
translations.idiomatic: That winter Qiang auxiliaries beat Han troops at Heluo—hundreds lost.

sentences.zh: 明年春，防遣司馬夏駿將五千人從大道向其前，潛遣司馬馬彭將五千人從間道沖其心腹，又令將兵長史李調等將四千人繞其西，三道俱擊，復破之，斬獲千餘人，得牛、羊十餘萬頭。

translations.literal: Next spring Fang sent Major Xia Jun with five thousand by the main road against their front, secretly sent Major Ma Peng with five thousand by a bypath against their core, and ordered chief clerk Li Tiao and others with four thousand to circle west—three routes struck together; he broke them again, taking over a thousand heads and a hundred thousand head of livestock.
translations.idiomatic: Next spring Ma Fang attacked on three tracks through Xia Jun, Ma Peng, and Li Tiao.

sentences.zh: 羌退走，夏駿追之，反為所敗。

translations.literal: The Qiang retreated; Xia Jun pursued and was defeated in turn.
translations.idiomatic: Xia Jun's pursuit failed.

sentences.zh: 防乃引兵與戰於索西，又破之。

translations.literal: Fang then led troops and fought at Suoxi, defeating them again.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Fang won again at Suoxi.

sentences.zh: 布橋迫急，將種人萬餘降。

translations.literal: Buqiao, pressed hard, led over ten thousand of his people to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Buqiao surrendered ten thousand followers.

sentences.zh: 詔徵防還，拜車騎將軍，城門校尉如故。

translations.literal: An edict recalled Fang and appointed him chariots-and-cavalry general while retaining his colonelcy of the gates.
translations.idiomatic: The court named him chariots-and-cavalry general and kept his gate command.

sentences.zh: 防貴寵最盛，與九卿絕席。

translations.literal: Fang's favor was highest; he sat on a separate mat from the Nine Ministers.
translations.idiomatic: He ranked above the Nine Ministers in imperial favor.

sentences.zh: 光自越騎校尉遷執金吾。

translations.literal: Guang rose from colonel of agile cavalry to Bearer of the Mace.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Guang went from colonel of agile cavalry to bearer of the mace.

sentences.zh: 四年，封防潁陽侯，光為許侯，兄弟二人各六千戶。

translations.literal: In the fourth year Fang was enfeoffed marquis of Yingyang and Guang marquis of Xu—six thousand households each.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year Ma Fang became marquis of Yingyang and Ma Guang marquis of Xu—six thousand households each.

sentences.zh: 防以顯宗寢疾，入參醫藥，又平定西羌，增邑千三百五十戶。

translations.literal: Because Emperor Ming lay ill Fang entered to administer medicine, and because he also pacified the western Qiang his fief increased by one thousand three hundred fifty households.
translations.idiomatic: Tending the sick emperor and pacifying the Qiang added thirteen hundred fifty households to Ma Fang.

sentences.zh: 屢上表讓位，俱以特進就第。

translations.literal: He repeatedly memorialized to yield office; both brothers retired as Specially Advanced to their mansions.
translations.idiomatic: Both brothers repeatedly asked to retire as Specially Advanced.

sentences.zh: 皇太后崩，明年，拜防光祿勛，光為衛尉。

translations.literal: After the empress dowager died, the next year Fang became superintendent of the imperial household and Guang commandant of the guards.
translations.idiomatic: After her death Ma Fang became superintendent of the imperial household and Ma Guang commandant of the guards.

sentences.zh: 防數言政事，多見采用。

translations.literal: Fang often spoke on government affairs and much of his counsel was adopted.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang adopted many of Ma Fang's policies.

sentences.zh: 是冬始施行十二月迎氣樂，防所上也。

translations.literal: That winter saw the first performance of the twelfth-month qi-welcoming music—submitted by Fang.
translations.idiomatic: The winter court music reform came from Ma Fang.

sentences.zh: 子鉅，為常從小侯。

translations.literal: Son Ju served as attendant minor marquis.
translations.idiomatic: His son Ma Ju was attendant minor marquis.

sentences.zh: 六年正月，以鉅當冠，特拜為黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: In the first month of the sixth year, because Ju was to be capped, he was specially appointed gentleman at the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Ju's capping brought appointment at the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 肅宗親禦章臺下殿，陳鼎俎，自臨冠之。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhang personally descended to the Zhangtai hall, set out cauldrons and stands, and capped him himself.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang capped him at Zhangtai with full ritual.

sentences.zh: 明年，防復以病乞骸骨，詔賜故中山王田廬，以特進就第。

translations.literal: The next year Fang again begged retirement on grounds of illness; an edict granted him the former Prince of Zhongshan's fields and mansion as Specially Advanced.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Fang retired ill with Zhongshan estates as Specially Advanced.

sentences.zh: 防兄弟貴盛，奴婢各千人已上，資產巨億，皆買京師膏腴美田。

translations.literal: The Fang brothers were magnificently wealthy—each owned over a thousand slaves and assets in the hundreds of millions; all bought fat farmland in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: They owned thousands of slaves and bought the best land in Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 又大起第觀，連閣臨道，彌亙街路，多聚聲樂，曲度比諸郊廟。

translations.literal: They raised vast mansions with linked belvederes astride the roads, spanning whole streets, gathering musicians whose tunes rivaled suburban temples.
translations.idiomatic: Their mansions and music rivaled imperial ritual.

sentences.zh: 賓客奔湊，四方畢至，京兆杜篤之徒數百人，常為食客，居門下。

translations.literal: Clients rushed in from everywhere—several hundred men such as Du Du of Jingzhao stayed permanently as house guests.
translations.idiomatic: Hundreds of retainers including Du Du lodged with them.

sentences.zh: 刺史、守、令多出其家。

translations.literal: Most provincial inspectors and magistrates came from their household.
translations.idiomatic: They placed kin as governors across the realm.

sentences.zh: 歲時賑給鄉閭，故人莫不周洽。

translations.literal: Yearly they relieved village kin—no old friend went unhelped.
translations.idiomatic: They fed neighbors each New Year.

sentences.zh: 防又多牧馬畜，賦斂羌胡。

translations.literal: Fang also pastured many horses and levied Qiang and Hu.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Fang ran vast herds and taxed frontier tribes.

sentences.zh: 帝不喜之，數加譴敕，所以禁遏甚備，由是權勢稍損，賓客亦衰。

translations.literal: The emperor disliked it and repeatedly rebuked them; restrictions multiplied until their power waned and clients faded.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang curbed them until their influence shrank.

sentences.zh: 八年，因兄子豫怨謗事，有司奏防、光兄弟奢侈逾僭，濁亂聖化，悉免就國。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, because of nephew Yu's slanderous letters, authorities memorialized that Fang and Guang lived beyond their rank, corrupted sage transformation, and all were stripped and sent to their kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: In year 8 Ma Yu's letters triggered charges of luxury; both brothers lost office.

sentences.zh: 臨上路，詔曰：『舅氏一門，俱就國封，四時陵廟無助祭先後者，朕甚傷之。

translations.literal: As they set out an edict read: 'The maternal kin all leave for their fiefs; none remains to assist at the seasonal tombs—I grieve deeply.'
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu's heir mourned that no Ma kin would tend the imperial tombs.

sentences.zh: 其令許侯思愆田廬，有司勿復請，以尉朕《渭陽》之情。』

translations.literal: Let the marquis of Xu reflect in his fields; authorities shall ask no more—this comforts my Weiyang sentiment.'
translations.idiomatic: He spared Ma Guang rent to soothe the bond of the Weiyang ode.'

sentences.zh: 光為人小心周密，喪母過哀，帝以是特親愛之，乃復位特進。

translations.literal: Guang was meticulous and careful; he mourned his mother excessively; the emperor therefore loved him especially and restored him as Specially Advanced.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Guang's grief for his mother won him restoration as Specially Advanced.

sentences.zh: 子康，黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: Son Kang served as gentleman at the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Kang served at the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 永元二年，光為太僕，康為侍中。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yongyuan Guang became grand coachman and Kang palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 2 Ma Guang became grand coachman and Ma Kang attendant.

sentences.zh: 及竇憲誅，光坐與厚善，復免就封。

translations.literal: When Dou Xian was executed Guang sat convicted of close ties and was again dismissed to his fief.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian's fall implicated Ma Guang.

sentences.zh: 後憲奴誣光與憲逆，自殺，家屬歸本郡。

translations.literal: Later Dou's slave accused Guang of plotting rebellion with Dou; Guang killed himself and his family returned to their native commandery.
translations.idiomatic: A Dou slave framed Ma Guang for treason; he killed himself.

sentences.zh: 本郡復殺康，而防及寥子遵皆坐徙封丹陽。

translations.literal: The native commandery then executed Kang, while Fang and Liao's son Zun were jointly transferred enfeoffment to Danyang.
translations.idiomatic: Local authorities executed Ma Kang; Ma Fang and Ma Zun were moved to Danyang.

sentences.zh: 防為翟鄉侯，租歲限三百萬，不得臣吏民。

translations.literal: Fang became marquis of Zhaxiang with yearly rent limited to three million cash and no authority over clerks or commoners.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Fang became marquis of Zhaxiang with capped income and no magisterial power.

sentences.zh: 防後以江南下濕，上書乞歸本郡，和帝聽之。

translations.literal: Fang later cited Jiangnan's dampness and memorialized to return to his native commandery; Emperor He permitted it.
translations.idiomatic: He asked to leave damp Jiangnan for home; Emperor He agreed.

sentences.zh: 十年，卒。

translations.literal: In the tenth year he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died in the tenth year.

sentences.zh: 子鉅嗣，後為長水校尉。

translations.literal: Son Ju succeeded and later served as colonel of the Long River.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Ju succeeded and became colonel of the Long River.

sentences.zh: 永初七年，鄧太后詔諸馬子孫還京師，隨四時見會如故事，復紹封光子朗為合鄉侯。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of Yongchu Empress Deng issued an edict summoning Ma descendants to the capital for seasonal audiences as of old and restoring Guang's son Lang as marquis of Hexiang.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongchu 7 Empress Deng recalled the Ma house and enfeoffed Ma Lang as marquis of Hexiang.

sentences.zh: 嚴字威卿。

translations.literal: Yan, courtesy name Weiqing.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yan, style Weiqing.

sentences.zh: 父余。

translations.literal: Father Yu.
translations.idiomatic: His father was Ma Yu.

sentences.zh: 王莽時為楊州牧。

translations.literal: In Wang Mang's time served as inspector of Yangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang he was shepherd of Yangzhou.

sentences.zh: 嚴少孤，而好擊劍，習騎射。

translations.literal: Yan was orphaned young yet loved fencing swordplay and practiced riding archery.
translations.idiomatic: Orphaned young, Ma Yan loved swordplay, riding, and archery.

sentences.zh: 後乃白援，從平原楊太伯講學，專心墳典，能通《春秋左氏》，因覽百家群言，遂交結英賢，京師大人咸器異之。

translations.literal: Later he informed Yuan and followed Yang Taibo of Pingyuan in study; he devoted heart to the grave classics, mastered the Zuo commentary to the Spring and Autumn, surveyed the hundred schools' sayings, thereupon bound himself to outstanding worthies, and great men of the capital all marveled at him.
translations.idiomatic: He then studied under Yang Taibo, mastered the Zuo tradition, read widely, and won the respect of Luoyang notables.

sentences.zh: 仕郡督郵，援常與計議，委以家事。

translations.literal: Served as county mail inspector; Yuan often planned with him and entrusted household affairs.
translations.idiomatic: As county mail inspector he counseled Ma Yuan, who left him family business.

sentences.zh: 弟敦，字孺卿，亦知名。

translations.literal: Younger brother Dun, courtesy name Ruqing, was also well known.
translations.idiomatic: His brother Ma Dun, style Ruqing, was likewise famous.

sentences.zh: 援卒後，嚴乃與敦俱歸安陵，居鉅下，三輔稱其義行，號曰『鉅下二卿』。

translations.literal: After Yuan died, Yan with Dun both returned to Anling, dwelt below the great tumulus; the Three Adjuncts praised their conduct, calling them 'the two ministers below the great mound.'
translations.idiomatic: After Ma Yuan's death the brothers retired below the great mound at Anling; locals called them the 'Two Ministers of the Mound.'

sentences.zh: 明德皇后既立，嚴乃閉門自守，猶復慮致譏嫌，遂更徙北地，斷絕賓客。

translations.literal: After Empress Mingde was established, Yan thereupon closed door self-guarding; still again feared drawing mockery and suspicion, thereupon further moved to Beidi, severing guests.
translations.idiomatic: When his niece became empress he shut his gate, then moved to Beidi to avoid scandal and cut off visitors.

sentences.zh: 永平十五年，皇后敕使移居洛陽。

translations.literal: Fifteenth year of Yongping: the empress ordered him to move his residence to Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongping 15 the empress ordered him to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 顯宗召見，嚴進對閑雅，意甚異之，有詔留仁壽闥，與校書郎杜撫、班固等雜定《建武註記》。

translations.literal: Emperor Ming summoned and received him; Yan advanced replies calm and elegant; the sovereign thought him extraordinary and issued edict to detain him at Renshou chamber, collating with collator Du Fu, Ban Gu and others the Jianwu annotated records.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming kept him at Renshou to collate the Jianwu annals with Du Fu and Ban Gu.

sentences.zh: 常與宗室近親臨邑侯劉復等論議政事，甚見寵幸。

translations.literal: He often with imperial kin Marquis of Linyi Liu Fu and others debated government affairs and was greatly favored.
translations.idiomatic: He debated policy with Liu Fu of Linyi and won imperial favor.

sentences.zh: 後拜將軍長史，將北軍五校士，羽林禁兵三千人，屯西河美稷，衛護南單于，聽置司馬、從事。

translations.literal: Later appointed chief clerk to the general—commanding five colonels of the northern army and three thousand Forest of Plumes palace guards—stationed at Meiji in Xihe—protecting the southern Shanyu—with authority to appoint majors and staff.
translations.idiomatic: As chief clerk he commanded northern-army and palace troops at Meiji to guard the southern Shanyu.

sentences.zh: 牧守謁敬，同之將軍。

translations.literal: Governors bowed to him with the respect due a general.
translations.idiomatic: Provincial governors treated him like a general.

sentences.zh: 敕嚴過武庫，祭蚩尤，帝親御阿閣，觀其士眾，時人榮之。

translations.literal: He was ordered past the arsenal to sacrifice to Chiyou; the emperor personally mounted the E pavilion and watched his soldiers—the age honored him.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming watched his troops pass the arsenal for the Chiyou rite—a signal honor.

sentences.zh: 肅宗即位，徵拜侍御史中丞，除子鱄為郎，令勸學省中。

translations.literal: When Emperor Zhang ascended he summoned Yan as palace attendant chief of secretaries, appointed son Zhuan gentleman, ordering him encourage study within the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang made Ma Yan imperial secretary and Ma Zhuan a gentleman-in-attendance for study.

sentences.zh: 其冬，有日食之災，嚴上封事曰：

translations.literal: That winter there was a solar eclipse disaster; Yan submitted sealed memorial saying:
translations.idiomatic: That winter's eclipse inspired Ma Yan's sealed memorial:

sentences.zh: 臣聞日者眾陽之長，食者陰侵之徵。

translations.literal: Your subject hears the sun is chief of the myriad yang; eclipse is sign of yin encroachment.
translations.idiomatic: The sun rules yang; an eclipse marks yin's advance.

sentences.zh: 《書》曰：『無曠庶官，天工人其代之。

translations.literal: The Documents says: 'Do not leave offices vacant—Heaven's work men substitute for it.'
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Documents says: fill Heaven's offices faithfully.'

sentences.zh: 』言王者代天官人也。

translations.literal: ' It speaks of kings substituting Heaven in appointing men.
translations.idiomatic: The king stands in for Heaven in staffing the realm.'

sentences.zh: 故考績黜陟，以明褒貶。

translations.literal: Therefore examine merit for promotion and demotion to clarify praise and blame.
translations.idiomatic: Merit reviews reward and punish.

sentences.zh: 無功不黜，則陰盛陵陽。

translations.literal: If meritless men are not removed then yin waxes and towers over yang.
translations.idiomatic: If useless officials stay, yin overwhelms yang.

sentences.zh: 臣伏見方今刺史、太守專州典郡，不務奉事盡心為國，而司察偏阿，取與自己，同則舉為尤異，異則中以刑法，不即垂頭塞耳，采求財賂。

translations.literal: Your subject humbly sees present governors and grand administrators monopolize province and commandery, do not devote themselves loyally to the state, while inspections bend partial—take and give at will—agree then praise as outstanding—disagree then strike with criminal law—or else hang head plug ears while collecting bribes.
translations.idiomatic: Governors favor allies, sell justice, and squeeze bribes.

sentences.zh: 今益州刺史朱酺、楊州刺史倪說、涼州刺史尹業等，每行考事，輒有物故，又選舉不實，曾無貶坐，是使臣下得作威福也。

translations.literal: Now Inspector of Yizhou Zhu Fu, Inspector of Yangzhou Ni Shuo, Inspector of Liangzhou Yin Ye—each time conducting merit reviews always there are deaths; moreover recommendations untrue—yet never demoted—that lets subordinates wield reward and punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Inspectors Zhu Fu, Ni Shuo, and Yin Ye kill men during audits yet face no demotion for bad picks.

sentences.zh: 故事，州、郡所舉上奏，司直察能否以懲虛實。

translations.literal: Former precedent: province and commandery recommendations memorialized upward—Director of Integrity examines competence to punish hollow praise.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly the Director of Integrity vetted recommendations.

sentences.zh: 今宜加防檢，式遵前制。

translations.literal: Now ought add guards and checks—model follow former system.
translations.idiomatic: Restore those inspections.

sentences.zh: 舊，丞相、御史親治職事，唯丙吉以年老優遊，不案吏罪，於是宰府習為常俗，更共罔養，以崇虛名，或未曉其職，便復遷徙，誠非建官賦祿之意。

translations.literal: Formerly chancellor and imperial clerk personally handled duties—only Bing Ji because old age drifted easy—did not investigate clerks' crimes—thereupon ministerial offices learned this as common custom—together deceiving and nurturing—to exalt empty fame—or not understanding their duties yet rotated elsewhere—truly not the intent of establishing offices and assigning salaries.
translations.idiomatic: Since Bing Ji's lax ministry, offices neglect duty and chase empty fame.

sentences.zh: 宜敕正百司，各責以事，州郡所舉，必得其人。

translations.literal: Ought order rectify hundred offices—each charged with tasks—province and commandery recommendations must obtain the right men.
translations.idiomatic: Charge every bureau; demand real talent from each nomination.

sentences.zh: 若不知言，裁以法令。

translations.literal: If they do not know their words—cut by statutes and ordinances.
translations.idiomatic: Punish ignorance of duty by law.'

sentences.zh: 傳曰：『上德以寬服民，其次莫如猛。

translations.literal: Tradition says: 'Highest virtue softens the people with leniency; next best is severity.'
translations.idiomatic: Tradition says: govern first with kindness.'

sentences.zh: 故火列則人望而畏之，水懦則入狎而玩之。

translations.literal: Therefore fire arrayed makes men look and fear; water weak lets men sport and play.
translations.idiomatic: Fire frightens; water invites contempt.'

sentences.zh: 為政者寬以濟猛，猛以濟寬。

translations.literal: Those who govern use leniency to aid severity and severity to aid leniency.
translations.idiomatic: Good rule mixes severity with mercy.'

sentences.zh: 』如此，綏御有體，災眚消矣。

translations.literal: ' Thus governed with proportion—disasters fade.
translations.idiomatic: Balance rule and omens fade.'

sentences.zh: 書奏，帝納其言而免酺等官。

translations.literal: Memorial submitted; emperor accepted his words and dismissed Zhu Fu and others from office.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang accepted it and cashiered Zhu Fu and peers.

sentences.zh: 弟敦，官至虎賁中郎將。

translations.literal: Younger brother Dun rose to colonel of the Gentlemen of the Household for All Purposes.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Dun reached colonel of the imperial guard.

sentences.zh: 嚴七子，唯續、融知名。

translations.literal: Yan had seven sons—only Xu and Rong were famous.
translations.idiomatic: Of Ma Yan's seven sons Ma Xu and Ma Rong stood out.

sentences.zh: 續字季則，七歲能通《論語》，十三明《尚書》，十六治《詩》，博觀群籍，善《九章算術》。

translations.literal: Xu, courtesy name Jize—at seven mastered the Analects; at thirteen clarified the Documents; at sixteen studied the Odes; broadly viewed many texts and excelled at Nine Chapters arithmetic.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Xu mastered classics young and excelled at the Nine Chapters.

sentences.zh: 順帝時，為護羌校尉，遷度遼將軍，所在有威恩稱。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Shun he served as Colonel Who Protects the Qiang and rose to General Who Crosses the Liao—wherever posted he won praise for awe and kindness.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shun named him Colonel Who Protects the Qiang and General Who Crosses the Liao.

sentences.zh: 融自有傳。

translations.literal: Rong has separate biography.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Rong has his own biography.

sentences.zh: 棱字伯威，援之族孫也。

translations.literal: Leng, courtesy name Bowei—great-grandson in the same lineage as Yuan.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Leng, style Bowei, was a collateral descendant of Ma Yuan.

sentences.zh: 少孤，依從兄毅共居業，恩猶同產。

translations.literal: Orphaned young, he relied on elder cousin Yi with shared livelihood—kindness like full brothers.
translations.idiomatic: He lived with cousin Ma Yi as brothers might.

sentences.zh: 毅卒無子，棱心喪三年。

translations.literal: Yi died without sons; Leng mourned three years with heart grief.
translations.idiomatic: When Yi died heirless Ma Leng mourned three years as for a parent.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：伏波好功，爰自冀、隴。

translations.literal: Encomium says: the wave-queller loved merit—from Ji and Long.
translations.idiomatic: The encomium: Ma Yuan sought glory from Hebei to Longyou.

sentences.zh: 南靜駱越，西屠燒種。

translations.literal: South he quieted Luoyue; west he slaughtered the burned tribe.
translations.idiomatic: South he pacified Luoyue; west he broke the Shaodang Qiang.

sentences.zh: 徂年已流，壯情方勇。

translations.literal: Years already flowed—heroic passion still fierce.
translations.idiomatic: Years rolled on; his courage never flagged.

sentences.zh: 明德既升，家祚以興。

translations.literal: Once Mingde ascended—the household fortune rose.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Ming lifted the Ma house.

sentences.zh: 廖乏三趣，防遂驕陵。

translations.literal: Liao lacked caution on three counts; Fang therefore grew proud and overbearing.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Liao stumbled three ways; Ma Fang turned arrogant.
